description:
P The Larger Prime is a card shedding game. The object of the game is to be the first player to ditch all your cards while playing prime numbers.

There are two set of rules, Normal Play and Tournament Play. If you are new to the game it is recommended that you play a few games using the normal rules to get a feel and understanding of the game before you try a tournament game. A Tournament game is not harder than a Normal game. It is just more complex and quickly becomes overwhelming because there are so many more possible plays. It may seem daunting at first but given time and practice it will become easier.

—description from the publisher

Trapped in a prison, you have an accomplice outside who gives you clues by smartphone. 60 enigmas approx. to solve by yourself or in teams. No game master.
—description from the publisher

The box contains a rulebook with 6 scenarios and the solutions, 10 envelopes, 60 enigma cards, a newspaper (Prison News), a planning guards and a map of the prison.

This game is inspired by the dancing plague that occurred in Strasbourg in 1518.

500 years later... the plague is coming back.

Strascendance is a placement board game where you are playing a killjoy who is trying to stop the progression of the dancing mania. With dices and pawns you will choose between protecting your places from the plague or bringing it to your enemies.

—description from the publisher

Emboldened by competitive gardening, your neighbourhood develops a new obsession:domestic fowl. You want to gather the most valuable flock, but your research has been, well, paltry. The market is unpredictable. Will it be chickens, turkeys or roosters that gives the greatest return? Discover how much each bird is worth, while building your collection. Who will win the
feathered crown and who will end up with egg on her face?

Each player acquires animals, with the aim of gathering a prize flock. However, the value of each animal type is hidden and can only be discovered by looking at information on the rule cards and animal cards. A player may always look at the rule card or
animal card in front of her. To look at the other cards, players must use the actions to swap rule cards or animal cards with those in the middle or pay another player to take a peek at her rule card or animal card. Players are allowed to write information down as they discover it. The player with the
most valuable brood at the end of the game is the winner.

—description from the designer

Word Chain is a party game in which players have to say a word that links to the previously said word, according to the rules shown on the cards they draw.

The game was originally an entry in the 2019 9 Card Game Print and Play Design Contest where it was voted best 3+ player game.

The Extended Edition of the game consists of 27 cards and does away for the need of any tokens to track scores. It features additional fun categories and includes variants for multiplayer and a solo variant that can be played in hand.

The Print and Play file includes optional card backs and a tuck box and is available from both PnP Arcade and Randomskill Games.
Professionally printed copies are available from The Game Crafter.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Bataille Empire: Wargaming Rules Revolution and Empire
description:
Bataille Empire is a set of rules for miniatures that allows you to fight battles from the French Revolutionary Wars and First Empire 1792-1815. Each player commands an army of twenty or so units.

The system allows you to play with miniatures of any scale 6, 10, 15, 20, 25 ou 28 mm.

The rules are compatible with all basing systems and therefore allow you to use your existing armies.

The rulebook for Bataille Empire also includes all the army lists (130) by period and campaign !

In the innovative Sequence of play, players play alternatively, activating formation depending on orders given, with the possibility of carrying reactions during the opponent turn such as formation change, counter-charge, opportunity fire. This results in a lot of interactions during the game.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Alubari: A Nice Cup of Tea – Heavy Work Gang
description:
Available initially at Essen 2019 as well as afterwards, from Heavy Cardboard.

When you have this piece of equipment, your third worker takes up two action spaces.

boardgame: Heroes of Tenefyr: The Second Curse
description:
Eerie stones have risen in the forest and a strange mist creeps in the valley. The village of Tenefyr seems to be cursed once again.

The Second Curse adds new heroes, new bosses and a lot more variety to Heroes of Tenefyr. Most of the new heroes are a bit less conventional than the original heroes, offering new unique ways to battle your enemies and work together.

With 130+ new cards, this expansion adds many new enemies, from creepy spirits to mechanical clockwork beasts, and mushroom creatures. Can you defeat them and lift the second curse?

—description from the publisher

As in La Fontaine's fable, players are frogs that wish to be as big as the ox.

All players must simultaneously collect or give frog cards to other players. When a player thinks he has reached the size of the ox, the round is finished and players may be awarded. To win medals, the size of the frog must be as close as possible to the size of the ox. But if you are over the size of the ox, your frog bursts and you have to give away a medal. The first player to have 7 medals win the game.

In Starforge, each player starts with an asymmetrical faction of constructible ships made from custom acrylic game pieces. In the center of the table is a row location cards that can be conquered and mined for resources. Through their choice of tactics, and how they build their ships, the players can battle and outwit their opponent achieving victory by command points, resource capture or enemy destruction.

Starforge is a PART-BUILDER game, where what you choose to build affects how the game unfolds.

—description from the publisher

Following the annexation of Texas by the United States in December 1845, war between the United States and Mexico became inevitable.  From 1846-1848, the two countries fought a bloody and bruising war culminating in Mexico surrendering significant territory to the United States.

The Halls of Montezuma tasks two players to recreate this pivotal war in American and Mexican history.  As the United States, you will be tasked with taking California and invading Mexico while facing mounting political opposition at home.  As Mexico, you will be forced to fight a defensive war of attrition against the better trained and led American troops.

The Halls of Montezuma is a low complexity, card driven game for two players (with solitaire rules). Players relive the decisions and dilemmas of this crucial period in history. Fast setup and a playtime of 60-75 minutes.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The devastating force of the eruption of Xitle completely buried and destroyed Cuicuilco and Copilco, both significant civic and religious cities. The survivors have scattered across Mesoamerica, many settling in and around Teotihuacan. The rapid infusion results in accelerated growth of Teotihuacan and leads to the adoption of new technologies as Teotihuacan establishes itself as a seat of power in the region.

Teotihuacan: Shadow of Xitle adds ten new technologies and ten new starting tiles to the game.

New technologies enhance advanced strategies and add incredible variety to the game experience by doubling the number of possible technologies. During set-up, you randomly choose six technologies as usual, ensuring that no two games are alike!

The new starting tiles offer new starting options for you to experiment with, further deepening your overall game experience!

All content is compatible with both solitaire play and the first expansion, Teotihuacan: Late Preclassic Period.

—description from the publisher

Are you ready for the test?

In Elemental Valley, players take the role of student elementalists. After years of study, they now face the final graduation test of the Academy.

In the test, each player is given a valley filled with elemental energies. The players will make use of them to construct and upgrade landmarks, activate landmarks' abilities, attack opponents and counter opponents' attacks. The first player who reaches the graduation Victory Points (VP) requirement earns the title of the Royal Elementalist and wins the game.

—description from the designer

Enjoy authentic cricket fun with this compact card and dice game. Take turns batting, bowling and rolling to see who can score the most runs from two quick innings. Fun and easy to play with just the right amount of strategy and surprise for cricket fans of all ages.

Each round the players takes on the role of batsman and bowler during an over. Each drawing 6 cards from either the stroke deck or bowl deck representing the 6 balls in an over. During each turn, the bowler plays a bowl card from his hand and the batsman responds with a stroke card. The result of the ball is resolved. When fielding is resolved, the top card of the fielding deck is revealed and the fielding dice is rolled to determine the outcome.

Game for two players, which represents the assault on the Mongo Planet by Flash Gordon and his friends. To achieve this, he must fight against Ming and his men, who are the inhabitants of that planet.

Flash Gordon and his allies have to move in their ships through space, and land at the bases of the Mongo Planet, occupying it to try to reach the city.

For their part, Ming and his men have to deploy their forces to prevent Flash Gordon from achieving his purposes. He can also choose to approach the base where your secret ship is hidden, and escape to escape to Earth.

In the course of the game both adversaries fight and capture prisoners, to facilitate the fulfillment of their respective objectives.

—user summary
Mini-expansion, consisting of one card that introduces the additional district „Freihafen“.

Devices is a quick, but strategic card game about building devices in order to fulfill customer orders. In devices, you gather resources and build machines that process and change those resources in order to fulfill different customer orders. Every customer order is worth varying amounts of points.

Customer orders are placed in the middle of the table to draft along with the "parts shop", where you get the parts for your devices. Players start off with basic machine parts and 1 cube of each color. The game is played over several rounds. Players can choose between several basic actions each round: collect cubes, buy cards (using cubes), accept customer orders, build parts from your hand on to your device, dismantle your machine, or load cubes into your machine and run it. Cubes that go into your device come out in various colors and amounts--depending on the machine you've built! You can then store those cubes or use them to fulfill a customer order and get points. The first player to reach 30 points wins!

—description from the publisher

A simple mini card game for 2 players, consisting of eight monster cards. The game is contained in Exit: The Game – The Haunted Roller Coaster.
Each player receives 4 monster cards. Then the active player calls a monster feature and its value (for example, 6 legs). The player with the higher value receives the top card of his opponent. Once a player has won all cards, the game ends.

—user summary
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Golem Arcana: Götze der Pravega Bonus Card
description:
Bonus Card for Golem Arcana

This ancient idol is Pravega, the faceless daughter of the Old-Venerable Allik, which must flow forever in front of her vengeful father.

Bonus:
If this Relict will be activated, add +4 Movement Points to the selected Golem, but also will loss 15 Healh Points. Discard after use.

boardgame: Justice League: Hero Dice – Superman: Smallville Promokarte
description: Promo card for Justice League: Hero Dice - Superman. Double-sided for use in either Solo or Team play.

boardgame: Similo: Fables – King Arthur Promo Card
description: Promo card from Spiel 2019. Later available during the Game Boy Geek Season 10 Kickstarter campaign.
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

boardgame: The Colour of Money Electronic Board Game
description:
One of twelve Money Target Amount Cards is drawn at random. These cards range in amount from £49,000 to £80,000. When playing multiplayer, the player to reach this target amount in ten turns or fewer is the winner. When playing solitaire, reaching this target amount in ten turns is a victory.

There are twenty Colour Money Cards  which represent the twenty cash machines from the British television game show. Each card represents a different payout ranging from £1,000 to £20,000 in £1,000 increments.

A turn begins by choosing a Colour Money Card and logging in the card’s number into the electronic Money Module. Every player puts one hand on the Money Module. It begins to count up from £1,000 in £1,000 increments. Any player who stops the module before it reaches its payout limit adds the amount when the device was stopped to one’s own score. Any player who waits too long receives nothing.

Even though the television series has one player playing against the house, this is a faithful adaption of the series.

—user summary

In the game Coffee Shop, the players have opened coffee shops right next to each other in the financial district and are competing to serve the same market.

Players draft ingredient cards and then customer cards.  Each round ends with a trading phase where players can trade money and ingredient cards with each other.

Each of the drafts also has an auction element.  While the active player chooses which ingredient they want from a face-up pool, the other players may bid higher and take it in an open turnless auction.

The customer draft works similarly, but with decreasing prices as the coffee shops lower their prices to attract the customers.

The game ends once a player has reached 10 or more points of customers.  Additional points can be earned by specializing in certain drinks, as well as cash on hand.  The player with the most points wins.

—user summary

Oom-Pah is a competitive speed game that combines Klondike Solitaire style columns, with snap style 'first in best dressed', with music theory.

You get a deck of cards with notes on them. You put four of them in front of you, as your 'Arrange Columns'. You put aside 8 of them as your 'Score Stack'. Finally, the remainder are placed next to your columns as your 'Impro Stack'. You draw from your Impro Stack in groups of 3 cards like Draw 3 in Klondike Solitaire. When there is a card in your Impro Stack or Score Stack that is the card under one of the ones on the top of your columns (like E is to F), then you can place the card there. Alternatively, if one of your cards in your Arrange Columns, Score Stack or Impro Stack is the next note in the Chord/Interval/Progression/Scale that you're playing with in the joint Compose Stacks, (like C is to A if you were playing Am (A-C-E)), then you can place it in the Compose Stack. It is not turn based - you work as quickly as you can, meaning that the faster player can place their card before the other. When a player empties their score stack, they yell 'Oom-Pah!' and get 10 points. Then each player gets points per the amount of cards that they played to the Score stack.

The game comes with 10 templates which have on them musical theory bits like Scales, Intervals and Chords. You can choose from any of them to play with, depending on the level of difficulty required, or what the players are trying to reinforce. So, for instance, you could play G Major, and require that players build G-A-B-C-D-E-F# in the score stack.

The game also comes with beginner, intermediate and expert rules which allow tailoring the experience.

From the publisher
Oom-Pah! For music nerds & legends… or those who wanna be! An addictive, fast-paced, brainfrying music theory card game that cleverly sneaks music knowledge into your brain!  
For 1-4 players, think classic card games like Speed/Spit (if challenging friends), or Patience/Solitaire (if playing solo), but instead you build musical sequences! 
Oom-Pah!'s unique colour-coded learning tools help you understand music concepts systematically. So whether you're an absolute beginner or musical maestro, you can compete at a level playing field. 
With over 1,700 different game options to choose from you’ll always be engaged, entertained, & highly entertained! Other than making you really smart and an awesome musician, the best thing about playing this game is the thrill of shouting “Oom-Pah!” when you win!

—description from the publisher

Did you travel with your truck all around Europe? It's time to thoroughly get to know Czech and Slovak trade cities.

Travel through our homeland and our eastern neighbors and earn the most money transporting goods. The richest truck driver wins.

Kamionem po Česku a Slovensku is a re-implementation of Auf Achse that takes place in Czech and Slovak Republic. It can be played on it's own with two players or used as an expansion to Auf Achse.

—user summary

There are 3 pieces to this cheeky character,
This makes her one of the simplest games to learn. However, don't be fooled! To win, one must be cunning and calculating.

This step-toothed figurine is designed to be aligned, so that the artwork matches up. Players take turns assembling at their choosing and then take turns at moving the head or the body. As both pieces have different cog notches they can only align on special numerical combinations. The seemingly straight forward nature becomes abundant, without seeing the paths to alignment an infinite game is possible!

The countenance bares resemblance to the prehistoric so-called Venus figurines, or could also be compared to Inca gold. Combined with authentic visage and mechanic rotatability Tressa has landed in the forefront of 21st century design! The etched tittering statuette is ultimately a tactile object: sits twisting ergonomically in the hand, mischievously perches on the desk, laughing about something no-one will ever know. Interestingly the form fits in the golden ratio - the height measuring approximately 1.618 of the width. The parts equally ascend in escalating measurements furthering this mathematic harmony.

The game has 3 steps to the head and 5 to the body. At any moment a player can call "Tressa" and change the lower direction, and the other player calling "Pympes" and changing the upper rotation.

—description from the designer
Guessing game with 1100 world records.

Naval Miniatures rules written for 400 ft to 1in ship models. These rules can be used for any theatre during the First World War and allow for all types of action although some, especially those involving submarines, ideally require an umpire.

—user summary

Experience Victoriana in a brand new way as you play through this narrative expansion.

Your team of investigators is assembled to solve the murder of a young woman. Has Jack the Ripper truly returned, or is there something even more sinister at work?

Told through prose, illustrations, and brand new game mechanics, the three act story of this expansion requires at least three games to complete and changes based on your success or failure. Once you've solved the mystery for good, there will be 12 brand new cards to add to the base game—including a brand new card type!

—description from the publisher

As the Avatar, it is your duty to serve the reigning Lord of Britannia. It is your passion to strive for mastery of each of the eight virtues. It is your quest to tame the dungeons, each representing its own sin.

The game consists of rounds, each round the player will do the following action items until the end game goals are reached.
 Roll four 8-sided die, and three 6-sided die [4D8 + 3D6] then: 

     Assign one D8 dice value to the list of CITIES. The number rolled represents the city which has called for aid. For example: if you assign a six,  Lord British has called for you to assist the city of Skara Brae. 
     Next, assign each of the three categories, THE TASK, THE VIRTUE, and, THE DUNGEON one of the remaining D8 values. For example: if you rolled a 2 and two 6s, you could assign the 2 to THE TASK, and each of the 6s to THE VIRTUE and THE DUNGEON. This would mean that the Avatar is to complete the task of Deliver a Message, meditate in the virtue of Honor, and battle in the dungeon of Shame. If the dice results do not allow for further progress, simply assign a dice to an already completed option and do not record any outcomes. 
     To settle the task, refer to THE TASK and TASK RESULT tables, assign one of the D6 values to the result and record the outcome on the play sheet. When a task is successfully completed, the Avatar gains favor with the city from which the task was given. Fill in one square for that city on the map portion of the play sheet to track the progress. If the task is failed nothing is recorded. When all four squares are full the city will offer the Avatar a gift which will provide advantages for the rest of the play, these advantages DO stack. It is not required to complete one of each task. 
     To settle the virtue, the Avatar simply meditates and gains one point in that virtue. Record the added virtue on the play sheet by filling in a single square next to that virtue on the right side. When all squares are filled, the virtue has been mastered. 
     To settle the dungeon, refer to THE DUNGEON and DUNGEON BATTLE tables. Dungeon battles will increase in difficulty as they progress; the last stage of the dungeon is the boss. When a dungeon battle is won, fill in a square next to the associated dungeon to show progress. If the battle is lost, no progress is recorded. The dungeon is considered cleared when the final square is filled in. Refer to the Combat section. 
     A second D6 value is assigned to a randomizer, refer to the RANDOMIZER table. 
     The third D6 is usually a wildcard but may be used as a task additional result assignment.


Fame and Karma are awarded or taken away depending on the results of THE TASK and THE DUNGEON. As the Avatar increases their Fame, they can use it to increase their power. For every 5,000 points of fame obtained, the Avatar can advance one stat to the next value shown. Refer to the Avatar section for more information. Karma can be traded for combat re-rolls or temporary bonuses, see Karma Allocation. Track the Fame and Karma in the allocated space on the play sheet paperdoll. Karma can be negative, but it does not affect the Avatar.

After all records have been kept and any combat is resolved, start the next round of play.

—description from the publisher

CasTiles and Armies is meant to be a fast-paced tile-based Castle and Siege strategy game for 2 players. Players will build a castle wall, reinforce it as needed, and attempt to defeat the enemy Monarch with their troops.

CasTiles and Armies consists of 2 phases. The Construction Phase and the Combat Phase.

The Construction Phase:
Starting with Player #1, the players will take turns placing castle tiles (dark grey) to construct their Castle Wall until it is 5-tiles wide. After placing a tile, the player may choose to discard one tile, placing it in a Rubble Heap, and then draw back up to 5 tiles in their hand. The Rubble Heap is a separate mass of tiles which can no longer be brought back into the game.

After the 5th tile is placed, 2 more tiles (a total of 6 additional tiles after the Gatehouse) may be placed in the Army Reserves Zone and/or the Offensive Line Zone. Be considerate of the numbers on the tiles during placement, the numbers all mean something! Once the castle is fully constructed, the final step in this phase is to calculate the strength of the wall. The Gatehouse icons count as wild cards and will conform to the number placed adjacent. We recommend placing dice at each of the 4 wall junctions with values that represent their strength. Dice make it easier to track damage on the castle wall. You will likely need a range of dice from D4s to D20s.

Tile placement is a key consideration! Your strategy during this phase of the game will determine your success as a defender or conquer. Tiles must be placed so they read left to right or top-facing-right (from the player’s perspective). See the possible scenarios below to determine how to calculate the strength. The higher the number, the stronger the wall. Once a tile is down it is down.

The Combat Phase:
With the castle wall now complete and total strength calculated and marked on the playing field, it is now time to start combat!

Each player should still have 5 tiles in their hand. At this time, ALL army tiles (gold-ish) in their hand can be placed in either the Army Reserves Zone or Offensive Line Zone, starting with Player #1. Hopefully, you didn’t discard them all during the last phase. Then discard or draw tiles as needed to keep only 3 tiles in your hand. Like the wall junctions, place a die on the arrow side of the army tile to indicate the strength of the army.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Imperial Settlers: Roll & Write – Halloween 2019 Promosheet
description: Imperial Settlers: Roll & Write – Halloween 2019 Promosheet gives you a fresh challenge for Imperial Settlers: Roll & Write.

DOVECOTE
Let the competition begins!
The flocks of pigeons are ready to fly off. Your goal is to land in the best boxes of the dovecote and to avoid getting stuck in the mud. Show your skills and lead your flock to the final victory.
Dovecote is a fun dexterity game for 2 to 4 players (6-year-olds and up) and with a duration of 15 minutes approximately.

In Dovecote, each player have a pigeons team. The players throw pigeon tokens to a 3D dovecote. The winner is who pass or put pigeons in the best places of the dovecote.

—description from the publisher (translated)

EL PALOMAR
¡Comienza la competición!
Los equipos de palomas están preparados para alzar el vuelo. Su objetivo aterrizar en los mejores lugares del palomar y evitar quedar atrapados en el barro. Demuestra  tu habilidad y lleva a tu equipo a la victoria final.
El Palomar es un divertido  juego de habilidad para 2 a 4 jugadores, a partir de 6 años y una duración aproximada de 15 minutos.

En El Palomar, cada jugador tiene un equipo de palomas. Los jugadores lanzan una ficha de palomas a un palomar en 3D. El ganador es quien pasa o pone palomas en los mejores lugares del palomar.

—description from the publisher (English)

This expansions adds more difficult challenges for Bilder.
It consists of 54 new 'WHAT' cards.

boardgame: Aladino y la cueva de las maravillas
description:
Relive the tales of the Arabian Nights together with young Aladdin in Aladdin and the Cave of Wonders. Go into the Cave of Wonders and get great treasures with the help of the Genie of the lamp, the Magic Carpet and Titi, Aladdin’s loyal monkey friend.

The board is setup wiht treasure pieces added to each space. Player roll the four sided die to move, collectin treasures as they go.  Bonus points are awarded for collecting sets or fofinishing a row or column.  The game ends when someone reaches 50 points, ends their board or cannot move. The player with the most Treasure points on their board at the end of the game, wins.

The game contains to cave of wonder board, players boards, treasure tokens and one pyramidal dice.

—description from the publisher (English)

ALADINO y la cueva de las maravillas
Revive la historia de las Mil y una Noches de la mano del joven Aladino. Adéntrate en la Cueva de las Maravillas y consigue un gran tesoro con la ayuda del genio de la lámpara, la alfombra mágica y tu fiel amigo mono titi. 
Ganará aquel jugador que, guiando al joven Aladino, consiga recopilar más puntos de tesoro en su tablero de juego al final de la partida. Para tal fin deberá explorar “La Cueva de las Maravillas” con la ayuda del dado piramidal.
El juego contiene un tablero de la cuevas de las maravillas, tableros de jugadores, fichas de tesoro y un dado piramidale.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
This military-themed expansion features not only the same Campaign Mode and Advanced Rules seen in Washington Z.C., but also a slew of new additions like a special Military Equipment deck, new regular Equipment and Pimpweapons to replace the core ones, military base tiles, veteran survivors, and, of course, zombie soldiers! All this comes to life in a brand new 10-mission campaign set in Fort Hendrix, a military base where mysterious experiments were being conducted in relation to the zombie infection.

—description from the publisher

Yatzy is one of the best-known and most popular dice games there is. Langland Yatzy allows kids to join the fun even if they cannot count yet, because here players use picture dice and try to roll long animals in order to score: Three of the nine dice each show heads, three dice show waists, and three dice show tails. As usual in Yatzy, players roll up to three times and can set aside or re-roll dice as they see fit in order to score the required combinations. And while players usually compete with each other, Langland Yatzy also offers a team variant.

Langland Yatzy is an easier accessible variant on the popular dice game that gets rid of the dice pips and replaces them with the cute, long animals from Langland. Players neither need to know numbers nor are they on their own, if they prefer to play the team variant.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The In-famous Gulch War of Gutterman's Gulch
description:
Inspired by Palm Island, this sequel to The In-famous of Gutterman's Gulch, is a hand held solo game where you must move up the gulch and battle seven enemy roosters who have a developed a thirst for war and total domination. Gather your ammo, recruit troops and make sure your army is strong enough to do battle. There will be skirmishes you will have to deal with, so stay under the shade of the trees. Watch out for snakes who love eating baby chicks. See how many roosters you can defeat. Add the Event Card to face even more difficulties. The "Absurd" Robot Expansion will give you two new, stronger enemies to battle with. Will you be the hero of The In-famous Gulch War of Gutterman's Gulch?

—description from the designer

Every player has 5 tiles with numbers from 2 - 6. On the other side of the tiles you can see a broken car.
The board is a circle with 16 fields, 8 in blue, 8 in red. Each player places his tiles on his half of the racetrack. The numbers are hidden, all tiles are revealed at the same time.
Red starts the game. A car is pulled as many fields as the number indicates. If this field is occupied, the opposing car is beaten and placed with the other side as a blockade on a free field on the racetrack. This field is no longer included in the setting.
It wins who has beaten first all opposing cars.

—user summary

A GAME OF STRATEGY
OBJECT: earn as many sea urchin tokens as possible.
To be able to catch sea urchins without pricking your fingers, you have to reposition them into very precise squares. Strategically place the pieces and anticipate opponents’ moves to win points!

+++  An exciting, dynamic game of strategy!

—description from the publisher

A GAME OF COOPERATION
OBJECT: place the tiles according to the wishes of each alien
All of the players play at the same time: they take care of their corner of the room (their board) and have to place “customer” and “order” tiles while respecting the restrictions of each one. They can interact and help one another to exchange tiles and fill their board. At the end of the round, when the hourglass runs out, everybody wins if each player has 10 points on their plate.

+++  4 levels of difficulty to season the game to taste.

—description from the publisher
8 promo cards released on Essen Spiel '19 with additional rules for the base game

The players try to get their officer from their bunker to the opposite one. Each player has 8 soldiers (small discs), 2 machine guns (big discs), 1 sergeant (small pawn) and the officer (large pawn). The pieces move and capture like follows:

soldiers: move and capture 1 space straigt or diagonaly forward
machineguns: move and capture over any unoccupied spaces diagonaly in any direction (like a bishop in chess)
officer: moves and capures 1 space in any direction (like a king)
sergeant: moves and captures over any unoccupied spaces in any direction (like a queen)

The first player to get his officer into the enemy bunker wins the game.

—user summary

Big Band is a Vinyl expansion that continues to build on the base game of Vinyl and introduces the Window Display, Hits Chart, and a new way to form collections. Historically, 78s and 33s were competing formats and available to purchase in the record store. In BB, players will focus on collecting albums from two genres -- Swing and Jazz. Players will acquire albums in two different speeds from up to 8 different band leaders. With respect to speeds, there are benefits in collecting 78s since they are worth slightly more than the 33s. However, the 33s offer collection powers to entice players to collect them instead. Furthermore, the rules for collecting albums are stripped down to just matching the genres. However, players must visit the front counter in order to earn collection rewards. To turn in a collection, the player must represent each of the four instruments in one genre. Depending on how many matches there are in the attributes will dictate what type of collection reward they will earn. Play continues until all the albums have been placed into the store. The player with the most points, in the end, is the winner.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Make It Dirty: The Game of Familiar Films Made Filthy
description:
Make It Dirty is a game of sinful cinematic parody that's only as dirty as you make it!

This box is stuffed to the brim with 200 classic movie titles, just waiting for you and your filthiest friends to pull one out and make it dirty! Your Director will pick the funniest, cleverest, or raunchiest new title you come up with—but each round only lasts one minute, so you have to go hard and fast if you want to leave everyone satisfied!

And if renaming classic films is just a little too vanilla, take on an extra-large challenge with a Make It Hard card, and keep the fun going all night long.

—description from the publisher

Havana Dice is a stylish Cuban twist on the traditional bluffing game of Liar's Dice, also known as Dudo. Roll the dice in this push-your-luck game that is at once fast-paced and easy-to-learn.

Using poker dice and unique dice cups, players roll their own dice in secret before bidding on the number of dice under everyone's cups—but if a bluff is successfully called, the bluffer loses a die.

Poker chips act as wild dice. A player may discard them when their bid is called to act as a die with the value of their bid. Once a call is made, but before players reveal their dice, the bidder must announce that they are using one or more of their poker chips and discard them. Every discarded poker chip counts as one die of the current value.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Camp This!: The Mint Tin Game
description:
Camp This! takes 2 players on a race against each another. Your goal is simple - complete your camp before the other player does. But beware! A group of neighboring campers will try to ruin both camps through a series of mischievous pranks.

You can construct your camp with six types of Resources - Campsite, Shelter, Fire, Food, Activity and Accessory. Neighboring campers will try to steal your food, get you into trouble with the local Ranger, replace your anti-bug spray with sugar syrup, set you on a fake Yeti hunt and do other pranks that will cost you victory points or Resources.

With the help of your bear token you can scare the neighboring campers away or you can even make them do damage to your opponent's camp.

Suitable for youngsters and adults alike, Camp This! comes in a tiny mint tin, which makes it easily portable anywhere you go.

—description from the publisher

A Kickstarter Exclusive stand-alone expansion for Hour of Need, giving you everything you need to play with 1-2 players ... or create a 1-6 player experience when combined with the Hour of Need core box!

This expansion adds 2 new heroes and their unique fixed decks to the roster: Gem and Ice! It also contains 1 new villain and their unique fixed deck, the nefarious Astasia! 2 brand-new issue decks await as well (complete with their own boards), providing you with exciting new locales to explore and schemes to thwart!

Also contains everything you need to play such as dice, clue cards and tokens!

A game that combines the role of werewolf and the move of a spy. the theme is a white room of nothingness, there is 3 faction in this game Villager, Hitman, and Serial Killer. Villager goal is to kill all Hitman and Serial Killer, Hitman goal is to kill all villager and serial killer, and serial killer goal is to kill hitman and all vilager.

The game play is that five people choose a color where it represent the pawns for the game. The arena consist of 36 rooms (6x6 room). The players then get the role. There is 5 role in this game

Gunner, fraction Villager. A villager that hold gun with 1 bullet if he meet serial killer or hitman he can kill them.

Ammo, fraction Villager. A villager that hold the amunition for the gunner, if he meet the gunner when gunner didnt have amunition he can reload the gunner ammo

Lazarus, fraction Villager. A man with 2 life if he died he can get revive. if he meet the same villager he can tell whether whom kill him.

Hitman, fraction hitman. A man with a gun if he meet villager other than gunner with loaded gun or serial killer he will kill him.

Serial killer, fracton serial killer. A man with a knife if he meet villager other than gunner with a loaded gun or hitman he will kill him

after the player get the role the player choose where the pawns will be placed

after that the player take turn rolling a 4 side dice. the number represent the room the player can move a room

if a player meet with another player in a room after he didn't have anymore room to move. the player that meet exchange the card and the rule of the game play

the game goes on until there is a fraction that win the game.

Place a tile on the board and use maneuvers to make as many matches of the target symbol as you can. You have 5 maneuvers per turn, in which you can rotate, slide, or swap tiles that are Active, meaning ones that have the same color adjacent sides touching. Think carefully about each maneuver though, because any Active Tiles left at the end of your turn will be worth negative points! Play it too safe, and you won’t make enough matches to score big points. Play it too risky, and you might end up losing more points in the process! The player that can think outside the box and attach the most matches will sweep the competition and win this easy to learn, hard to master puzzle game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Wander: The Cult of Barnacle Bay – Cola the Soda Bomber

A strange explorer from a far-away land, Cola found that his skills were more than welcome in the company of the Wanderer's Guild.
He uses his vast alchemical knowledge of soda-bombs to support his fellow Wanderers and hinder his enemies!

boardgame: Wander: The Cult of Barnacle Bay – Eileen the Assassin
description:
Eileen, a well-trained assassin and hunter of great renown, was a natural fit for the Wanderer's Guild. She prowls the lands, searching for any who are tainted by corruption of any form. She is vigilant and careful not to become corrupted herself, else she'll be hunted next...

Rayna, Mary Fixit, and Thalia represent some of the top talent the Wanderer's Guild has to offer!

Rayna, the most physical of the three, is a dragoon extraordinaire, who gracefully leaps through the air and wields her spear like the masters of old.

Mary Fixit is a talented Golemancer who's intelligence and ingenuity are unparalleled within the Guild.

Thalia comes from a long line of toxiphiles who specialize is potions and brews as well as how to deliver them with a bow.

Add these three heroes to your adventures in Barnacle Bay to change the tide and defeat Elder Bane and his Cult of the Deep!

Various event cards color the game.

description:
This is an expansion for Dwar7s Winter, a cooperative "tower defense" game in the Dwar7s family.

This expansion adds new rules and 3 legendary classes of dwarves, as well as compatibility with Dwar7s Spring (includes "The Legendary Conversion Pack").

The giant monster Varanor has been defeated, but he was only the beginning... The lab techs have analyzed the mutations of this monster and it is unlike aynthing seen before. It appears that Varanor was exposed to a new form of alien radiation were calling "Nova".

We've traced Nova to a small but fortified enemy research base, and our intel reports that they are preparing to launch a missile that will spread Nova across the globe creating millions of super monsters. We don't know their motivations, but you must stop that launch at all costs...

Adventure scenario expansion for MechAge.


The scenario consists in 1 Scenario card and 2 Mech cards:

     Rules booklet
     Countdown Scenario card
     Twin Cannon Mech card
     Personnel Carrier Mech card


Players are expected to provide their own tokens for this scenario:

     1 Countdown token

Released in the July 2019 Board Game of the Month Club $10+ package.

boardgame: Death Derby: A Post-Apocalyptic Card Game
description:
The year is 2200 and survivors of man-made and natural disasters struggle to live in a world poisoned and devastated beyond recognition. The new extreme sport and source of income for many are Death Derby races in which competitors vie to win or just to survive.

Death Derby features random courses formed from 15 track sections, 12 vehicles that can be upgraded with 37 different modifications, and 40 action cards that can influence the outcome in multiple ways.

Be the first to finish the race or the last one standing as you try to out think, out race, and out shoot your opponents. Modify your vehicle into a death machine or speed demon to survive while hampering everyone's efforts with well timed action cards.

Can be played in a group from a single deck or a single modified deck, or you can modify your own to take on someone else's.

One 10-sided die, tokens, and pen and paper are needed to play.

—description from the designer

Temporal agents (historians) from competing academies have traveled back in time to witness the sinking of the Titanic and correct any discrepancies in the historical record. Shortly after arriving, the Titanic's collision with the iceberg shatters their time machines, scattering the components and destroying the time modules. Luckily, the remaining components can be assembled into a Transporter that will allow an agent to leave the ship for ... somewhere else on Earth. Realizing they are trapped in 1912, the temporal agents decide to rescue as many passengers as possible who would otherwise have died - 1,517 people. The effects of such a rescue on the timeline are hard to predict...

Escape from the Titanic is a board game where players attempt to construct a Transporter and rescue as many doomed Titanic passengers and crew as possible. But, they must do so before the ship tilts to 90 degrees and sinks!

The game comes with two expansions already included. The optional components allow players to also collect Titanic artifacts and rescue castaways that were brought to the ship when the temporal agents' time machines were shattered. You will encounter such castaways as Albert Einstein, King George V, and Thomas Edison along with other famous people who were supposed to be on the Titanic but missed the trip such as Milton Hershey and J. P. Morgan.

—description from the publisher
family: History: The Sinking of the Titanic

description: Nightshade is a single-player game focused on writing a plot for a noir investigative story. The main character in each story (chosen and played by the player) is an investigator, some times a professional and some others a complete amateur. Players must put together all the clues and characters involved in the story to create a coherent plot on a violent mystery!
1-3 player WW2 sniper board game! As an American Sniper you'll sneak behind enemy lines, complete objectives and snipe Nazis!

boardgame: Milites Mundi: Ancient and Medieval Wargames Rules for Smaller Scale Figures
description:
Milites Mundi is a 'big battle' games rule set for fighting actions in the ancient and medieval periods.

Games of Milites Mundi involve hundreds of figures per side arranged into multiple units.

The game is designed to last for two hours and encourages the use of historical tactics and formations (without being prescriptive) and emphasises the the need to maintain cohesion in the army's battle line and sweeping flank attacks.

—description from the publisher

description:
Coliseum: Choose Your Weapon is a competitive card game for 2-4 players. Each player assumes the role of a Mighty Gladiator. You must prepare yourself and utilize your impressive skills in combat to defeat equally formidable opponents. All the while you are both bolstered and terrified by the ever-growing tension and excitement of the crowd; baying for blood, and the sweet relief of victory to fill their pockets with gold. You could be their reigning champion, you cannot disappoint them, or it will cost you your reputation… and your life.

When opening the Coliseum box, the player will find a simple to play but complex to master game which is, above all, very intuitive. With Gladiator, Skill and Equipment Cards, you quickly prepare your fighter. Then, the dice and strategy guide the player during combat, which usually ends in slaughter or technical defeat after about fifteen minutes.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cold Confusion: The Soviet Raid on Iceland, 1985
description:
“In combat, despite the most careful preparation, risks cannot be avoided. Attention must therefore be given, and the most detailed plans prepared, for every reasonable exigency of the overall operation.” –Excerpt from the syllabus for Soviet officer training at the Frunze Academy.

“There are no impossible tasks.” –Motto of the Soviet 106th Desant Division.

An attack on NATO’s air and naval installations near Reykjavik, Iceland was assumed should war between the alliance and the Soviet Union break out. The airfield at Keflavik played a crucial role in Operation Reforger which would rush vital resupplies and reinforcements from the US and Canada to Western Europe. The naval installation near there monitored the underwater SOSUS line that monitored Soviet submarines in the North Sea and North Atlantic. However, most US and other NATO military leaders assumed a Soviet attack would be in the form of air attacks by long range bombers with cruise missiles and conventional bombs. However, an airborne and amphibious assault could not be ruled out. This designer has made some assumptions in crafting this game:


     The Soviet High Command mobilized a reinforced regimental-sized (331st Regiment of the 106th Desant Division as well as Spetnatz commandoes) airborne and amphibious assault that would be launched at the very outbreak of the war.
     The Soviet assault seek to inflict as much damage as possible on the NATO military installations in and around Reykjavik and Keflavik, as well as gathering any intelligence materials discovered during the raid.
     Evacuation and even the survival of the raiders would be secondary to the successful elimination of the NATO installations.


The game lasts up to 12 turns. Each turn represents six hours of time. A hex is about 6 kilometers across. Combat units are companies.

—description from the publisher
Squad add-on for the Darkness faction. Contains cards for solo, cooperative, and PvP.

ELEMANCERS: Light and Dark is a 2-6 player team-based fantasy area control strategy game in which you take control of an Avatar and Clan wielding 1 of 6 elemental magics: Dark magic (Shadow, Necro, or Ice) or Light magic (Celestial, Beast, or Flame). Using these powers, you summon creatures, cast spells, activate enchanted weapons, and invoke creature abilities.

You play as part of 1 of 2 Factions (Tenebris = Dark Faction or Lux = Light Faction) warring head-to-head which are trying to take control over the Realm by either having the most elemental power, or by finding lost lore and artifacts in order to learn how to resurrect their Primal God of elemental magic at the Hollow of Fate.  This location being the place where the gods were last seen after their epic battle, as well as where the dragon Baransu is currently residing, waiting to awake to rid the world of elemental magic and the Factions of Light and Dark!

The game is made up of 2 Rounds, with each Round having two phases: Activate and Conflict. During the Activate Phase, all Clans obtain more Battle Cards, Power Tokens and Creatures. During the Conflict Phase, Clans take turns spending a Power Token to either summon creatures, draw more Battle Cards, utilize their Avatar’s power, or activate a found Legendary Weapon. After spending a Power Token, the Clan may then move any creatures of theirs on the gameboard, followed by an option to Battle at any contested locations. Creatures in the game have a variety of different traits that can be used in Battle, and Battle Dice, Creature Traits and Battle Cards are used in Battle to defeat the enemy!

In the course of the game, Clans complete Saga Cards (secret objectives) to find Lore throughout the Realm and finally a Legendary Weapon’s location and use. These artifacts provide a power boost in the game and help satisfy one of your Faction’s win conditions. Lore Cards provide the history of a Legendary Weapon and story beats encountered at different triggers in the game. Clans are also trying to control matching element locations to place more Power Tokens on the gameboard in order to have the most elemental power to win!

—description from the publisher

In タピる (Tapiru), players attempt to build three cups of bubble tea, with consistent branding, in an effort to be the first player to collect scoring tiles with the "Ta" "Pi" "O" "Ca" sounds.

Specifically, three columns are created between the players, one for each cup.  Each column gets a random scoring tile, and a token to indicate whether the scoring tile will be awarded to the cup with the most or the least tapioca. Each player starts with a straw and bottom for each of their three cups.

On a player's turn, they will draw a tile from the stack and then play one tile to one of their cups.  The tiles may be a tapioca card, a branding card (the cup logo), or a bottom.  If a bottom card is placed, that column's demand switches, either from most tapioca to least tapioca, or the reverse.

At the end of a round, cups will be eligible to score if they have a lid, bottom, three tapioca sections, and a brand.  Players each have a total maximum amount of tapioca that may be contained in their three cups.  The player who best satisfies each columns' requirements wins the corresponding sound tile and additional may be earned if all cards of one cup contain the same color of straw.  Multiple rounds of play may be required to determine a winner.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The rules for Animal Inc describe it as an "unusual semi-cooperative hand management game".

The game consists of 36 cards in 5 suits, with numbers ranging from 0 to 12, and each suit has a unique distribution.  For instance, all grey cards are 6's, and green cards are numbered 3-9.

Cards are dealt out evenly, with each player then choosing a few cards to discard.  The players are adding cards in a pyramid structure that is 8 cards wide on the bottom.  To play a card on the bottom row, a player simply plays it next to a previously played card.  To play a card on a higher row, it must be supported by 2 cards, and at least 1 must be the same color as the card being played.

The game ends when all players are out of cards, or no player may legally play another card.

If the pyramid reached the 5th tier (4 cards wide), then the last player scores positive points equal to the sum of the values shown on all cards in the 5th tier and above; all other players lose points equal to their single highest card.  However, if the pyramid does not reach the 5th tier, the last player loses points equal to their single highest card, and all other players gain points equal to their single highest card.

The player with the most points after a number of rounds wins.

—user summary

The Marquis is a 1-4 player thematic cooperative stealth game which takes place in 18th century France and which will let you experience the adventures from historical novels in the mysterious town which is hiding its dark underground secrets.

A group of three young aristocrats yearning for revenge come to the gloomy streets of mysterious town Montbeliard. 
The players will experience the underworld of aristocracy, full of intrigues, lackeys and traitors and reveal the 
hidden mystery of Count Ressequie´s family.

The Marquis is a Stealth game. Light is your enemy, dark and shadow is your friend. Quietly approach the guards while hidden in the shadows, jump out of the dark corner to unarm the enemy, or surprise them with resolute attack by the sabre. Hide into the shadows, climb on the town towers to reach open window, many times the only way how to get into building. Shoot your shiny copper musket from the tower to cover your comrades. 
Mysterious black fog coming from the sewers hides monster known as the tongue of Mephistopheles somehow connected with the count's family.

The Main Story Line is uncovering slowly as you play and consists of 12 chapters plus side quests.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Carmen Sandiego: ACME'S Most Wanted Board Game
description:
Based on the new animated series from Netflix® , race around the world to deduce the Loot stolen, the Location it was stolen from, and the VILE. Henchman who did it! Features incredible artwork from the show. Learn about Locations and Loot while you save the day!

Play as Carmen Sandiego as you travel to exotic locations on the hunt for the lost loot.

From Italy to Australia, to the coasts of San Francisco, you will need to examine the cards from your fellow players to deduce the who, what, and where of this criminal affair!

Think you know who stole the loot and where? Hustle over to the Isle of VILE. to announce your suspicion and stop the bad guy in his tracks!

Based on the incredible new animated series from Netflix, Carmen Sandiego: ACME’s Most Wanted is the mystery solving game you’ve been waiting for."

In a variant on Clue, players attempt to determine which VILE Villain, Loot, and Location cards are hidden in the Confidential Envelope by travelling the globe as an ACME Agent. At a location, a player announced their SUSPICION (I.E. which VILE Villain and Loot are attached to that location) and if the player to the left has any cards to disprove that, (s)he secretly shows them to that player.

Play continues as such until a player, through the process of elimination, believes they have the correct answer. They make a formal ACCUSATION, and look at the cards in the envelope. If they are correct, they win the game. If not, they are out of the game and play continues.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

boardgame: Doctor Who: Exterminate! The Miniatures Game – Missy & the Cybermen
description:
This expansion adds Missy and a group of cybermen to Doctor Who: Exterminate! The expansion includes one Missy, twelve Cybermen, and fourteen cybermat miniatures, ten faction cards, and eight new adventure cards to expand the Doctor Who: Exterminate! universe.

—description from the publisher

Five Sisters is a game developed according to the same name novel from Taiwan, as in Chinese "有五个姊姊的我就注定要单身了啊".

Chasing the five sisters, you will move among the three locations on the board to seek every chance to gain favorable degree with the characters. When you have enough favorable degree towards one certain character, you can show your love to her, and whether you'll success or not depends on the result of dice roll. If you succeed, you'll gain victory points.

In this game, you can show your love to different sisters simultaneously and even if any character has been shown love by other players, do not worry, you may have a duel with them to retrieve the situation!

—description from the publisher

Special Card for Llama

The No Drama Lama is put in the middle of the table at the beginning of every round. Once you pass, take the card. If another player already has the card, take it from him. Only the last remaining player can't take the card.

When the round is over, by usual rules (everybody has passed or someone played his last card), the owner of the No Drama Lama may discard one card before counting his points. If that card was his last card, he can discard one token (black or white).

boardgame: Arena: The Contest – Hydra, Ascaran the Archangel & Vanarus the Demon
description:
One Boss and two heroes exclusive to Kickstarter.

The Hydra is a 90x120x85mm miniature (3-inch base diameter). It comes with a special one-shot Boss Fight cooperative quest, to be played after the events that end the Epic Campaign.

Ascaran, the Archangel, and Vanarus, the Demon, are two special Kickstarter-Exclusive Heroes from a seventh Combat Role, the Bruiser, that is not currently present in the Core Box or Expansion Box.

The thick forest north of Arendelle is known as a magical place. Since the Forgotten War, the inhabitants of the kingdom have been trying to avoid it. All local paths lead to the dense thicket, and elemental spirits glide amidst its branches and leaves.
Get ready for a big trip! You have the honor of accompanying Anna, Elsa and their friends on the way north. Will you be able not to lose them in the enchanted forest, or will the ancient powers make you wander in magic circles?

The rules are explained in 5 minutes! 

     Play Movement cards and move your piece
     Reveal Characters and resolve their effects
     Remember where your Characters are during other players’ turns as they will get flipped over at the end of their turns
     Whoever reveals 6 Characters of their color, wins! 


—description from the publisher (English)

Холодное сердце 2: Заколдованный круг

Дремучий лес к северу от Эренделла прослыл волшебным местом. Со времён забытой войны жители королевства стараются обходить его стороной. Местные тропы норовят завести с опушки в густую чащу, и меж ветвей и листьев скользят духи стихий.
Готовьтесь к большому путешествию! Вам выпала честь сопровождать Анну, Эльзу и её друзей по дороге на север. Сможете ли вы найти их в волшебном лесу, или древнее волшебство заставит вас бродить по заколдованному кругу?

Простые правила объясняются за 5 минут

     Играйте карты движения и перемещайте свою фишку
     Открывайте персонажей и разыгрывайте их действия
     Запоминайте местоположение своих персонажей
     Первый, кто открыл 6 персонажей своего цвета, побеждает!


—description from the publisher (Russian)

boardgame: Холодное сердце 2: Зачарованный лес (Frozen 2: Enchanted Forest)
description:
Elsa, Anna, and their friends are headed as north as they can go. A mysterious forest lies ahead where they will face danger and unexpected encounters. Help them find their way through the enchanted forest and overcome all obstacles! 
Frozen 2: Enchanted Forest is a card-placement family game where you will be building a common path of cards with matching symbols. The first player to run out of their cards wins!

—description from the publisher (English)

Холодное сердце 2: Зачарованный лес

Эльза, Анна и их друзья отправляются в далёкое путешествие на север. Перед ними лежит таинственный лес, где героев ожидают опасности и неожиданные встречи. Помогите друзьям найти дорогу в зачарованном лесу и успешно преодолеть все препятствия.

—description from the publisher (Russian)

Willyrex, Vegetta777 and their friends wake up in a mysterious cave without knowing how they got there. But, they are not alone... inside the cave there lurk some dangers.
With the help of the Goblins, they will try to come back home a loot of gems, while avoiding falling into the traps or being caught by the Hypnocielagos or the Slimo King.

Can you help our friends to escape from the caves and collect the most valuable gems?

On each turn, players must play a creature card from their hand. All cards are revealed at the same time and the solved its effect. First to play is the slimo kings. They will catch the Hypnocielagos in play. Then, if there are hypnocielagos in play, they will catch the goblins in play. Last, if there are goblins in play, they will collect the gems from the cave. Each type of creature will be solved starting with the card with the highest value.

Collected gems and trapped creatures you get along the game will give victory points. At the end of the game, the player with the biggest loot will be the winner.

—description from the designer (English)

Willyrex, Vegetta777 y sus amigos despiertan en una misteriosa cueva sin saber cómo han llegado hasta allí. Pero no están solos; las cuevas están repletas de vida y con dentro acechan grandes peligros.
Con la ayuda de los Duendes, intentaran volver a casa con las valiosas gemas de la cueva, evitando caer en las trampas y ser atrapados por los Hipnociélagos o el Rey Slimo.

¿Podrás ayudar a nuestros amigos a escapar de las cuevas y reunir la mayor cantidad de gemas?

En cada turno, los jugadores deben jugar una carta de criatura de su mano y deberán revelarla a la vez. Primero se resolverán los reyes slimo que haya en juego. Estos atraparán a los Hipnocielagos. Luego, si hay Hipnocielagos en juego, atraparan a los duendes de la cueva. Y finalmente, si hay duendes en juego, podrán recolectar las gemas de la cueva. A su vez; cada criatura se resolverá comenzando por la de mayor valor en la carta.

Las gemas conseguidas y criaturas atrapadas de darán puntos de victoria. Al final de la partida, el jugador con el mayor botín será el ganador.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

description:

boardgame: SAC: All Out Nuclear War Against the Soviet Union, 1962
description:
"SAC: All Out Nuclear War Against the Soviet Union, 1962" is an introductory level, low complexity wargame simulation. It portrays a “what if” attack by the US Strategic Air Command (SAC) against the USSR. The premise of the game is that the Cuban “missile crisis” escalated to an all-out nuclear war between the Cold War protagonists. The game is designed for two players, one taking on the role of the attacking US forces and the other the defending air defense forces of the Soviet Union. The game can also be played solitaire. The SAC game's design is based on the classic NORAD game, published over four decades ago by Conflict Magazine and Simulations, and designed by Dana Lombardy. My design is a tribute to that classic, but with the gaming perspective "switched" and some new rules "tweaks".

—description from the publisher

In Quota, players play a game within a game, anytime, anywhere. Simply give each player a card with two quotes, which they have to sneak into conversation without getting caught. Play during game night, a dinner out, a camping trip, and more. Each quote is worth points based on its difficulty. The winner at the end of the night then gets an agreed-upon prize.

Playing is simple- draw a card with two quotes, keep them secret, and carry on with your evening. Then, when the time is right, sneak a quote into conversation. If you get caught, then get a new card. If you don't, and the conversation carries on, show your card and get the points, drawing a new card. What will your score be at the end of the night?

description:

Players begin the process by planting trees, which may grant them ownership of some of the orchard. Owners earn money whenever someone uses one of their spaces. Taoex is an abstract strategy game where players use mono-directional tiles to navigate a hexagonal grid to capture opponents’ tiles to create multi-directional towers for greater mobility. Using the 6-pointed compass, that control all direction of play, located in the center of the board, players take turns placing their 12 tiles (2 for each compass direction) in the optimum starting hexes. Once all the 36 starting hexes have been filled the game begins with the players taking turns advancing at least 1 tile into play per turn. Within the board there is a 6-pointed star called The Way where tiles are forced to roll the 10-sided Taoex die to charge the path a tile/tower moves across the board. Regardless of whether the roll is beneficial to the rolling player, the tile must move the roll along the Way if it can. Tiles/towers that traverse board to the far side of the board, if the tile/tower manages to line up with a starting hex, the tile/tower can wrap around the board to come out on the other side once again. An excellent tactic when hunting new directions.

As players begin collecting enemy tiles, by landing on them, they form towers of up to 6 tiles tall, maximum. Anything over 6 tiles the tower’s owner either removes enemy tiles from play or in the case of his own, liberates them and returns them to an open starting hex of the appropriate direction of the tile. The game is won by a player collecting 6 of one enemy’s tiles into a single tower or, if 1 player captures/eliminates all opponent tiles.

Taoex is a challenging and unique abstract strategy game that is very simple to learn and yet offers the players a mental workout as they master their play. Naturally, the rules that govern the game are more complex than the description written above, but it is the nuances in the rules that makes all games more interesting and Taoex offers its fair share. If you are the kind of gamer that enjoys using your mind to defeat opponents rather than just relying upon some lucky dice roll or random chance, then Taoex is a game for you.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Edge: Dawnfall – Plastic Bosses Expansion
description:
Co-op and PvP Expansion! 6 bosses for all the main factions! Additional cards and scenarios!

A.I. Bosses:
Take part in the special dedicated solo system where player controlled factions fight against Huge Bosses controlled by A.I.

Each boss has its own unique set of actions on cards and each game order of those cards change Boss tactics. Each Boss is different and you always play against one at a time.

While fighting against boss player take 3 cards from its deck and create its action deck. Each card has 2 actions, top and bottom. When cards are lying on table, bottoms of higher cards cover tops of lower cards, so initially boss uses only 4 actions. When you wound boss enough times it does not dies, you simply remove its action card revealing previously hidden action. Then you have to wound new card until moving to next. Boss dies only when you remove its final card.

Boss actions are very unique and full of flavor to each Boss. Each action also has different targeting system, it can target weakest models, closest, all base to base, etc.

Each boss also has its own set of tokens like Quarry, Control, Shields, Toxic that gives it the additional twist to its mechanics and combat characteristics.

Additionally, each boss has 2 mini scenarios.

Every single Boss brings tons of fun and challenge to encounters and will require many attempts to beat them!

Also, each Boss now will belong as fully playable Boss for its respective faction. Players will be able to field them in battles against other players and you can expect full asymmetry and unique flavor for every single boss just like in A.I mode.

Fancy playing as Captain Solo and the Rookie or as a Super Soldier and his side kick...you can now!

A competitive board game about driving through the city where the roads make no sense and everything is always changing on you. Players build and modify a road network across the board based on a real-life city (Boston, in this edition).

Roads are represented by intersections on hex tiles and can be laid out in any space and orientation available on the board. Players can drive across any road, no matter who laid it.

The first player to get to their randomly-chosen destinations wins.

—description from the designer

Unfundable is a 5-player card game about the competitive yet collaborative world of academic research. It is played in 3 free-form rounds of negotiation. Players negotiate over the division of resources required to obtain a grant, as well as the division of the resulting rewards.

Players balance short-term collaboration with the long-term need to achieve their secret goal and avoid becoming Unfundable, i.e. having the fewest Papers at the end of the game.

Unfundable was designed as an engagement tool between researchers of various levels, from early-stage college students to postdocs. But it is also a quick, casual game that anyone can enjoy. It requires no background knowledge in research or tabletop gaming.

—description from the designer

AARGH! It is a competitive card game based on the live role-playing events of Survival Zombie, where each player controls a group of survivors whose only mission is simple: get the seals that will make their group escape from the zombie apocalypse. However, the different groups must face zombies, test and incidents using their skills while meeting different characters.

The game consists of 200 cards and markers and offers 3 different types of games:

Survival: Game of 2 to 8 players, initially they will give each player 3 survivor cards and one of modifier.  Get 2 stamps to win the game, make it difficult for opponents because only one team will escape the apocalypse.  Maximum of 6 cards in hand.

Cooperative: Game of up to 8 players per team, initially 2 players of survivors and a modifier card are given to each player.  It consists of playing in the same way as the Survival game, the teams will always be alternate at the game table and the objective will be to get 3 stamps per team.  The modifying cards are still secret to the rest of the players, among teammates you can only ask if they have cards of a certain type but not how much they reward or penalize.  Maximum 5 cards in hand.

Sudden Death: A game of up to 16 players, initially 1 survivor card and a modifier card will be delivered to each player.  Special survivors' cards and encounter cards must be removed from the event deck, as getting stamps is not essential but to survive.  Alliances are prohibited, so you cannot help a rival unless you get a benefit from it.  Maximum 3 cards in the hand.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – BARC Speeder Unit Expansion
description:
Versatile and swift, BARC speeders are designed to assist clone troopers as they scout the field, protect key targets, and launch rapid surprise attacks. These durable speeder bikes are armed with heavy blasters and fitted with powerful engines, making them useful in nearly any theater of ground war across the galaxy.

Within the BARC Speeder Unit Expansion, you’ll find one unpainted, finely sculpted BARC speeder miniature identical to the miniature included in the Star Wars: Legion Clone Wars Core Set. If you choose, this miniature can include a sidecar featuring a built-in M-45 Ion Blaster or a Mark II Medium Blaster. Similarly, a Phase I Clone Trooper can fire an RPS-6 rocket launcher from the sidecar. This expansion also includes a unit card and an assortment of six upgrade cards inviting you to outfit your speeder to fit your personal battle plan.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Droidekas Unit Expansion
description:
Versatile and extremely destructive, Droidekas are a threat to even veteran squads of clone troopers and skilled Jedi Knights—and when B1 Battle Droids fail, droidekas are often sent to execute their primary function: annihilation of their targets. Rolling swiftly into battle, droidekas unleash their arm-mounted twin blasters, pouring out withering torrents of fire.

The Droidekas Unit Expansion lets you support your droid forces with some of the most powerful combat droids in the galaxy. Four finely detailed, unpainted Droideka miniatures identical to those found in the Star Wars: Legion Clone Wars Core Set represent your Droidekas as they speed across the battlefield in wheel mode and unleash suppressive fire in their attack configuration. Meanwhile, a unit card fully outlines the Droidekas abilities while three upgrade cards give you the freedom to modify them with comms systems.

description:
Inspired by tabletop classics like Dungeons & Dragons and Magic: The Gathering, Veil of Ruin is a progressive deckbuilding RPG.

Veil of Ruin is an immersive gaming experience designed to give you hours of unique and ever-changing enjoyment. Thanks to Veil of Ruin’s quick setup and simple, yet intricate combat and dungeon crawling systems, you and your friends can go from opening the box to slaying monters within minutes. Venture into the dangerous, dying jungles of Orman, taking on the persona of one of our six unique Heroes as they fight their way through a myriad of Enemies.

Gameplay:

Veil of Ruin features three modes, Skirmish, Into the Jungle and PVP.

Skirmish games are a cooperative one time experience for 1-4 players that take between 45 minutes and 2 hours.  Select the difficulty, pick the monsters you are fighting, draft your deck, get some equipment and then fight!

A more intense experience is our Into the Jungle mode, a roguelike adventure designed with replayability in mind. Each cooperative session for between 1-4 players takes between 2 and 5 hours, players draft cards, gain items and other loot as they progress through the jungles of Orman. 
 PVP is best played once you have a good understanding of the flow of gameplay.  With a few tweaks of the rules 2-4 players can battle one another to determine who is the champion of the jungle.

—description from the designer

Una Vittoria Inutile, Goito 1848 (A Useless Victory, Goito 1848) is a wargame by designer Andrea Brusati about the attack of the Austrian Army of Field Marshal Joseph Radetzky against the Piedmontese I Army Corps, deployed to protect the bridges over the Mincio near Goito, during the First Italian War of Independence.

Una Vittoria Inutile, Goito 1848 is the second wargame of a series, using the same rules system, named “Risorgimento”.

The Operational Game System “Risorgimento” simulates the battles and campaigns of the Wars of the Italian Independence (1848 – 1866) at regimental and battalion level. The system does not deal with the tactical formations of the period, instead it highlights the command systems and capacities, the fog of war, and the consequent operational and strategic decisions, keeping the game complexity at middle to low level.﻿

Move your feet to get a seat!

It’s a fun, active game! Fess up (or brag!) about things you’ve done, stuff you like and things you wish! All players but one sit in a chair, while one player stands in the middle and reads one of the prompts from the device, such as “Butts Up if you have gone zip lining.” If that applies to you, get up and race to another chair someone has vacated! The player left without a chair is the new one in the middle! When a person becomes the middleman, they take a rubber ring (included). The end of the word is just the beginning

Using the letters laid out in the middle, spell a word along the rim of the disc! Build off of the letters the previous player used, so you score more and make them lose points! If the previous player spelled GAME, and you spell MEETING using their M and E, you cause them to lose two of their points, and you get seven (one for each letter)! If the next player spells GASP using your G, you lose one of your points and they get four…and so on! Use a little strategy when you spell, trying to make it difficult for the next player to steal more than one of your letters. As letters from the middle are used, replace them from the extra pile—so your letter choices are always changing! It’s a great game for all levels of spellers.

boardgame: Irregular Wars: Conflict at the World's End – A Wargaming System for Small Actions in the Age of Discovery
description:
Irregular Wars: Conflict at the World’s End provides a fun and easy, fast-play, set of rules for engagements on the fringe of the European world – Ireland, the Americas, the East Indies and the vast steppes of Central Asia. The lands discovered by European explorers seeking easy access to the trading wealth of the Indies exposed untold avenues for expansion. The sixteenth and seventeenth centuries saw successive waves of self-aggrandising traders, raiders, conquerors and colonists leave Europe for new lands beyond the sea. Some pursued the freedom to practice persecuted religions or the opportunity to convert the heathens, others sought raw resources in the form of gold, furs or exotic spices or plants, like nutmeg or tobacco. The game is designed for engagements between two forces of from 500 to 2,500 fighting men per side. Each player represents a leading conquistador, noble, governor, daimyo, chief, cacique or petty monarch. These leaders are given the umbrella title of ‘lords’. The key to success in Irregular Wars is the lord’s ability to harness the enthusiasm of volunteer or militia soldiers which, together with the effective use of terrain, counts for more than sheer numbers alone. The uncertain nature of army recruitment and variable level of centralised command and control make the rules ideal for small scale, localised and asymmetrical warfare. The game could quite easily be expanded to cover earlier or later conflicts anywhere in the world prior to the establishment of reliable and professional armies. This second edition of Irregular Wars continues the fun and fast approach of the original rules. More background information and explanations are provided and the phase sequence has been streamlined. Additional changes throughout the rules make the randomised aspects of the game more realistic… in a random sort of way. This edition includes army lists for 48 different sixteenth and seventeenth century armies belonging to the major European colonial powers and the indigenous populations of Ireland, the Americas, East Indies and Eurasian Steppe.

—description from the publisher
