description:
Ravage - Swamps of Delgor is a 1-5 player dungeon crawl. You don’t play the heroes in this game you play Orcs, Goblins, Gnolls, And Kobolds of the Blackroot clan. Your chieftain has your orders you will seek glory and smash the faces of your enemies for teef.

Explore the treacherous swamp and the ancient temple with your friends by drawing map cards, level up skills and buy goods with teef. Watch out for elites and heroes that wander the land as well as completing your objective.

Featuring:
Co-op
Play with up to 4 friends with an automated overlord.
Solo play
Play as 2 characters through quests.
One vs many
Play with up to 5 friends 1 taking on the role of the overlord. This can be a much more grilling experience depending on your Overlord.

Ravage - Swamps of Delgor is the sequel to Ravage - Dungeons of Plunder, it is a stand alone game and does not require the original, however they are completely compatible and can be combined to give you more items, monsters and characters.

—description from the designer
family: Theme: Wetlands / Swamps / Bogs / Fens / Marshland

description:
BREVITY ASSAULT contains an Operational Level that permits to play a larger scale battle than the most diffuse campaign games available for ASL. Basically what you get is a game at company/platoon level where you solve the combat not with simple dice rolls but with ASL scenarios. BREVITY ASSAULT depicts the hard fought battles that occurred in mid May, 1941 on the border between Lybia and Egypt during the so called "Operation Brevity".

BREVITY ASSAULT includes the following:

7 action packed scenarios, including 3 campaign games (CG) covering the Operation Brevity from 5 to 18 operational turns
    30 full color, die cut 1/2" ASL counters
    31 full color, die cut 5/8" ASL counters
    217 full color, die cut 5/8" operational counters
    One 8.5"x 11" operational map which represent historical situation on the Lybia/Egypt border
    Two 30” x 26.5” HASL map sheets which combine to represent the historical layout of Halfaya Pass
    One 31” x 23” HASL map sheet which represents the historical layout of the village of Sollum
    Rules pages describing Vehicles/Ordnance notes, BA Scenario Special Rules (SSR), map terrain and campaign game rules
    A divider card for quick reference of SSRs during scenario and CG play

Ownership of the following Hasbro/MMP ASL products are required to play all of the included scenarios: Beyond Valor©, West of Alamein©, Hollow Legions©

This historical module for Advanced Squad Leader covers main episodes of the Luftwaffe Panzerdivision Hermann Göring counter-offensive in the Gela-Scoglitti sector in Sicily, following the landings of the 1st US Infantry Division "Big Red One" and the 45th US Infantry Division "Thunderbird", in days 10th, 11th and 12th of July 1943.

BIAZZA RIDGE includes the following:

10 action packed scenarios, including 1 campaign games (CG) covering counterattack battles around Gela, Sicily
    20 full color, die cut 1/2" ASL counters
    16 full color, die cut 5/8" ASL counters
    One 38.2” x 35.9” HASL map sheet which represents the historical layout of Biazza Ridge
    One 37.2” x 17.8” HASL map sheet which represents the historical layout of Abbio Priolo (Hill 41)
    One 32.5” x 20.9” HASL map sheet which represents the historical layout of Gela Farello
    Two 20.9” x 19.5” HASL map sheets which represent the historical layout of Piano Lupo (Road Junction Y) and of Ponte Dirillo
    Rules pages describing BR Scenario Special Rules (SSR), map terrain and campaign game rules

Ownership of the following Hasbro/MMP ASL products are required to play all of the included scenarios: Beyond Valor®, Yanks®, Hollow Legions®, boards 19 and 69, overlays OG1-5 and O1-2

MOD is a trivia game supported by a mobile application that contains questions in five different categories with two different game options.

The game features a mobile device, double-sided gaming platform and five pawns in different colours. The current category and difficulty on the platform are selected in the application to see the question to be answered. The game platform consists of three rings and is based on the level of difficulties with the questions.

Each round, the player is asked three questions. Players will receive a random category and wildcard by pressing the dice button in the app and select a difficulty level of their choice. Each well-known question is advanced by one household, by medium household by two households, and by difficult households by three households.

—description from the publisher
publishers: Koz Oyuncak Sanayi ve Ticaret Limited Sirketi

boardgame: Materia Prima: The Alchemists Seal Expansion
description:
The all-dominant race for the Philosopher‘s Stone causes some alchemists to use dirty tricks. With the expansion, alchemists can block hexes with Seal Stones, occupy element mines, and build a resource monopoly. The Expansion also adds new alchemists, homunculi and equipment cards to the base game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Return To Sender: The Mail Game
description:
Also styled as “Return To Sender” The Mail Game and The New Post Office Game!

This appears to be a reasonably straightforward 'roll and move' style game - or more accurately 'spin and move' since a spinner is provided with the game - it is assembled in the centre of the board itself.  It contains a fold out board, instructions, and playing pieces in (at least) five colours - one for each player.  It is themed on the delivery of mail through the US postal service.

Players each start their letter tokens on the "Sender - start here" space on the outermost coil of the spiral, and move around a spiral course of 61 spaces, until reaching one of the blue "Mail delivered - game won" space.  Unusually, the innermost coil of the spiral contains five of these winning spaces (interspersed with hazard spaces), and there is one more winning space at the end of the second coil.  There are two kinds of hazard spaces (yellow = lose a turn; red = go back), as well as green 'do nothing' spaces, and further blue spaces on which nothing happens when landed upon, but which serve as location markers for some of the 'go back' hazards.

The box states "A Thomas Game - manufactured by American Game & Dice manufacturing Co., Cleveland, Ohio", and the rules leaflet says "A Thomas Game, 4191 Weymouth Road, Medina, Ohio, 44256-9247".

—user summary
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
Maximillian is a tabletop vehicle combat game set in a dystopian 1930s World. Players battle with heavily armed cars and bikes amid the roar of engines powered by alcohol and aviation fuel!
Power-slide and corner your way around the table and/or terrain, by using a special cornering template. Driving skill, the size and handling of a vehicle, and vehicle’s speed influence success or failure in a turn or manoeuvre.
Get your vehicle ready and we’ll see you out there on the roads, driver! Tally-Ho!

—description from the publisher

Just Don’t Call Them Dark Elves!

Far beneath tortured Perdition lays a honeycomb of twisting turnnels and deep caverns. Venture from a dark and Hidden Realm to brave the blinding light of The WitchBorn ravaged world. These are the Daughters of Twilight.

—description from the publisher

Carbonfighters is a resource management racing board game for 2-6 players simulating a five Grand Prix formula racing season during which you constantly develope your racecar and pitcrew while competing to be the champion by winning the most GPs.

Grands Prix take place on five different modular tracks made up from hexa tiles. As the season evolves tracks become more and more complex. There are two kinds of resources: tyres and fuel. You spend them according to the racetrack tiles you move onto and the development state of your vehicle. You need to pick a driver too who’s resource saving abilities you fancy.

At the beginning of each Grand Prix you choose from two strategies. According to your decision movement cards are dealt to you with movement points on them to move forward on the track. Your strategy decides if you can use higher or lower numbered movement cards.

Qualifying determines the starting order of the players’ cars. A Grand Prix consists of rounds until someone reaches the final tile. Each round consists of the following steps:

     Choosing a card or using the slipstream card.
     Spending resources 
     Resolving action situation when cars are in front 
     Announcing pit stops to refill your resources and activate driver abilities


After the Grand Prix is finished medals are given to the first three players. All players get also victory points according to how many tiles they have reached. These are for developing your car and/or pitcrew before the next Grand Prix.

—description from the designer

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Neutral Heroes II
description:
Many Figures in Westeros do not Work for one Faction Alone

Westeros is a land not unfamiliar with war and conflict. Much blood has been shed all across its expanse. During times of strife, there are those that don’t simply stand behind a single banner, but will move from force to force looking to either make some coin or simply follow their own paths and desires. These individuals are no less useful on the battlefield, as they often bring special skills that may not be found under one’s own banner.

The Neutral Heroes II Box has many of these individuals, from all walks of life and from all corners of Westeros. There are new Unit Attachments such as Bronn or Brienne. There’s new Non-Combat Units like Jaqen H’ghar and Tycho Nestoris. There are even new Neutral army commanders like Vargo Hoat and Daario Naharis, complete with their Tactics Cards. Each one can dramatically alter the way an army functions on the battlefield.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Neutral Stormcrow Mercenaries
description:
With war happening regularly in Westeros, certain groups have formed around the idea of selling their armed services to anyone who can pay their price.

The Stormcrow Mercenaries are one such group. This unit has moderate stats, but is a perfect addition for players who like Unit Attachments, as their special rules let them take one for fewer points than usual.

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Neutral Stormcrow Archers
description:
The unrest in Westeros has created a large market for men willing to sell their services to any House willing to pay.

The Stormcrow Archers unit box gives commanders a dedicated ranged attack unit for their forces. Their Longbow attacks can be used to thin out enemy ranks from a distance. Being mercenaries has also made them thrifty, receiving a one point discount on one Unit Attachment that commanders assign to them.

—description from the publisher

Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

description:
Home Port is a shipping themed board game, designed to bring people together around a journey of fun, concentration and celebration. Home Port incorporates economic principles such as supply and demand, cash-flow, buying and selling of both natural resources and containerized goods, as well as various real-life applications affecting the world of shipping and international trade. The pick-up-and-delivery mechanism adds an exciting flow to the game, while the systematic movement of sailing ships across the map creates an awareness of physical and time constraints. The game plays 2-5 players for about 60-90 minutes with the aim of acquiring 7 Game Point Tokens first, combining strategic planning, fleet expansion, and financial acceleration.   
All aboard!! Home Port is shipping in some serious fun!

—description from the designer

6つの祭壇 (Muttsu no saidan) The Six Sacred Altars is a Japanese game for two players. During the game, players play cards to the table, trying to control the total value in play. The deck contains number cards and action cards which affect the results.

Each player is dealt 6 cards to their hand. They then take turns to either play a card face up or discard a card (drawing a new card from the deck each time), or discard all six cards and redraw.

Once six cards have been played, the round ends and the total worked out from the cards on the table.

If the total is equal or over 10, the start player (attacker) scores 2 points. Otherwise, the second player (defender) scores 1 point. The players now swap roles of attack and defence and continue.

The first player to win a 2 point margin wins the match.
user summary

A Card game, two players compete changing offense and defense on each round. Each player plays a card from hand alternately. Once six cards has played, a winner will be chosen according to the sum the numbers on them. The action cards will be the key to victory!
It's a compact but full-of-compete-feeling game.

攻守交代しながら戦うカードゲームです。
手札を交互に出し6枚目が並んだ時点の場の合計値で勝敗決定します。
アクションカードが、場を揺るがす勝敗の鍵に！
コンパクトながらしっかり、バトルしてる感を味わえます✨

Links Promo is a cooperative trivia game with 13 Clue cards and 14 Number cards.
The goal is to Link all the Clue cards to the Number cards on the table. One Number card will remain. Validate its code with the Answer card to know if you win this level.
There are 2 levels in the game.

Chop Chop Ninja Junior is a spin on the classical card battle game with graphics from the Chop Chop Ninja TV Series.

Each player turn its card. The highest wins the hand. If its a tie, a battle begins. The goal of the game is to get all the cards.

boardgame: Génies en Herbe: Les vrais génies

Trivia games where players are split in two teams. Each team tries to be the fastest to give an answer but if it's wrong, the opposite team gets a right of reply. Questions themes goes from school subjects to public figures passing through sports and general knowledge.

Questions come from the 2017 provincial Génies en herbe tournament in  Thetford Mines, Québec, Canada.

boardgame: Le jeu questionnaire RPM: L'univers de l'automobile
description:
RPM is a trivia game where you answer question from one of 5 categories: Car race, Who am I (car parts), Jet Set (famous cars and people), Technology and History.

The game progresses on a board where each square indicates one or two question category from which you may choose. You move on the board using die. There are also some bonuses found on the board to help you get to the end faster than your opponents.

boardgame: Speak Out: All About Sports & Games
description: All About Sports & Games adds 144 new phrases to Speak Out.

This addictively entertaining game is an excellent conversation starter and a great way to muse over your life experiences, relive forgotten memories and humorously revisit your youth! You Don’t Know Me! will revolutionize your social get-togethers forever! This multi-generational game makes sharing YOU--your interests, your likes and dislikes, and your life experiences--with your friends and family exciting and fun! Learn to know the people in your life in a way you never thought possible! With over 500 questions to choose from You Don’t Know Me! probes into the depths of each player and breaks down the walls of conversation to find out exactly who does know you! You Don’t Know Me! is a must-have addition to any game collection--perfect for game nights, family reunions, holidays, teachers, ice-breakers, slumber parties, co-worker functions, or couples outings!

Zonkers is the fast and frantic dice game. Players are given 5 dice of the same colour and 6 cards are laid out in front of them.

The head-to-head style play starts with players frantically rolling their dice and matching the number with the correct dice graphic on the cards. When the cards are filled with dice, or when a player places their last die, the round ends and the player with the most dice on a card wins.

The total game time is 15-30 minutes and there are 6 different variations to try. Great travel game to keep kids entertained.

—description from the publisher

A Mountain Adventure in the Swiss Mountains. It's a funy Dice Game about the huts in the Swiss Alps.

—user summary

Ein Bergabenteuer in den Schweizer Alpen. Spannendes Würfelspiel für zwei bis sechs berg- und hüttenbegeisterte Spielerinnen und Spieler ab acht Jahren. Wer schafft es am schnellsten, in jeder der sechs Bergregionen seine Hütte zu besuchen? Neben guter Ausrüstungsplanung und dem nötigen Wetter- und Würfelglück ist richtiges Verhalten im Gebirge und in den Hütten hilfreich, um das Abenteuer gut zu bestehen. Dazu erfährt man viel Interessantes über die einmalig gelegenen Hütten des Schweizer Alpen-Clubs und Privathütten in den Schweizer Alpen. In brenzligen Situationen greift auch der Rega-Helikopter ins Geschehen ein. Viel Spass auf der grossen Hüttentour!

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Unternehmen Rösselsprung: Caccia a Tito – Drvar 1944

"Unternehmen Rösselsprung: Caccia a Tito - Drvar 1944" is a tactical wargame which simulates the operation Rösselsprung (Knight's Move), codename for the avio operation acted by 500th SS Para Battalion to chase Marshal Tito in May 1944. 
Each point is 40-50 men and each turn is about 2 hours each.

This wargame was sent as a gift to the subscribers of the wargame magazine "Para Bellum".

boardgame: Hail the King: F'r evil shall cometh
description:
A fierce two-player card game to save the kingdom of Samavia from an imminent evil that has risen to ravage and claim all.

STORY:
It revolves around the medieval times where a heinous and wicked creature has risen.

The old prophets of Samavia have foreseen this crisis and reckon that a battle between many Guilds or Houses of the kingdom must take place to find the One that will face, fight, lead and hopefully triumph over the beast(s).

HOW TO PLAY:

     First player draws 3 cards while the second draws 4 cards
     Choose a location or the enemy to conquer - Press your luck with dices - strategize using the cards and give yourself an edge.
     Resolve the battle and place your token on the territory
     Win by conquering 3 territory

ブラウメア (Buraumea) BLAUMARE is a Japanese co-operative word game.

Mito is trapped in her bad dream. To escape from it, she must gather images of the reality by asking herself how the reality looks like. But the nightmare might be showing her false images to misdirect her.

In the game of BLAUMARE, players divided into two forces. One player is the nightmare who try to misdirect opponents. The others represent one girl named Mito who is trying to escape from her dream. Mito's side wins if they successfully make three pairs of cards of different symbols.

At the beginning of the game, players are secretly assigned their roles. One player is the nightmare, and the others are Mito. Each player is dealt a certain number of image cards and one clue card. Such as 'number', 'color', etc. is written in clue cards. If you have 'number' clue card, you may only ask about numbers, such as "is the reality related to six?"

When you try to make a pair, you try to find another player who has the identical image of reality (namely, who has the identical card as one of yours). First, you ask everyone else what the reality is related, complying with your clue card. Other players say "Yes" or "No". Then you target a player who said "Yes". Both you and that player place the card which they think is identical with each other face-down.

In a later turn, you may reveal the two cards. If these are identical, Mito's side takes the pair which can count toward victory. Otherwise the nightmare takes these two cards.

Mito's side wins when they successfully make three pairs of cards with different symbols. The nightmare wins when they have taken three pairs of cards with different symbols or six cards with the same symbol, or when the image deck runs out.

As the nightmare, you should try to misdirect Mito's side indicating that you have the right image, even if you don't have. But you shouldn't be obvious.

description:
サマータイムメモリー (Sama- Taimu Memori-) SUMMER TIME MEMORY is a memory game with card-specific effects. Its theme is enjoying summer vacation.

At the beginning of the game, ten cards are placed on the table face-down.

During your turn, you draw a card from the top of the draw pile. If the card has special effect, you may reveal the card to activate the effect. Otherwise place it on the table face-down.

Then you reveal two cards on the table. If these cards match, you take them and score victory points. Repeat the process until you reveal different cards.

The game ends when three cards "お", "わ", and "り", which form the word "おわり (end)", are revealed, or when the draw pile is depleted. The highest scorer wins the game.

boardgame: Black Powder: A Dark and Bloody Ground
description:
Miniature Wargame rules for the French and Indian War.

Fought between 1754 and 1763, this conflict saw the French and British empires fighting for dominance of Canada and North America. The European armies had to overcome harsh weather conditions and the equally unforgiving wilderness before fighting battles and skirmishes in which little quarter was asked for or given.

Includes complete history of the conflict with details of every major battle fought between the two sides. Also included are details of the uniforms, organisation and commanders of the European armies, their colonial regulars and militias, and the various Native American tribes that supported or opposed them. Scenarios are provided for many of the battles of the war along with suggestions for campaign games.

boardgame: アニマルウォーズ (ANIMAL WARS)
description:
アニマルウォーズ (Animaru Wo-zu) ANIMAL WARS is a 2-player card game with a theme of animal survival.

There are 18 animal cards in this game, each of which has its own strength and special power. Players take four cards at beginning of the game.

Each round, players place their first cards in front of them, then reveal them. Then do the same for the second card.

Then players take turns choosing to play an additional card or not. This round ends when all players pass in a row.

Each time a card is revealed, if the card has 'When revealed' effect, resolve it immediately. Some cards have continuous effect which is active while revealed.

At the end of the round, you win a round if your total strength is greater than your opponent's. You score two victory points by winning the round. Some cards give you extra points if they survived the round.

The game ends when a player has accumulated five victory points. The highest scorer wins the game.

description:
Tabletop wargame for mass battles, historical or fantasy. The 2 versions of the rule share the same principles for orders, combat and fire.

3 levels: from tactical to strategical.

Standard : ~13 units / ~1h 
Epic : ~30 units / 2h 
Legend: ~50 units / 3h

BannerWar is a fluid and balanced system. It incorporates combat and fire resolution suitable for tactical battles.
On a strategical level, the rules add orders and deployment principles.
It takes into account a wide variety of troops while keeping the rules simple.
Its army lists offer real options on a tactical as well as on a strategical level.
Faster than most, it does not require more figures than other rulesets.

—user summary

BannerWar comprend une version de règles Historique et une version Fantastique. Les deux versions utilisent les mêmes mécaniques de jeu pour les ordres, les combats et les tirs, cependant la version historique est plus détaillée sur la formation des armées, les manoeuvres stratégiques et la psychologie ; alors que la version fantastique est plus accentuée sur la diversité et l'originalité avec quelques règles spéciales qui donnent beaucoup de caractère aux unités.

La version historique offre des champs de bataille variés et très agréables, qui rendent les parties fortement stratégiques. Elle apporte la possibilité de garder des troupes en réserve, en renfort, en embuscade ou de les mettre en avant garde ou arrière garde. Cette version est accés sur le réalisme grâce aux règles d'ardeur et de résistance. Les unités se déplacent et combattent de manière historique sans être noyées de règles spéciales.

La version fantastique est allégée sur la stratégie des terrains et des corps d'armée. Les contraintes de déplacement et certaines phases de jeu sont moins détaillées pour faire place à des règles sur les Monstres, les Créatures Volantes, les unités mythiques, les Héros et la Magie.

Bien entendu comme le système de jeu est commun, il est tout à fait possible (et même recommandé à terme) de jouer la totalité des règles de BannerWar. Cela ne rend pas les parties bien plus longues ni moins fuides; et cela permet d'avoir un jeu historique ou fantastique d'un grand réalisme avec une diversité de troupes trés élevée qui se joue autant sur la partie tactique que stratégique.

—description from the publisher

Too Many Ghosts! mixes the collaborative experience of Pandemic and Forbidden Island with the mounting pressure and complexity of Oregon Trail, and the set-collection mechanic of Sushi Go. It's an explosive recipe that will have friends and family coming together to battle ghosts to score the best payday possible.

So if you're into mischievous ghosts, making bank, or just getting along at game night, then give Too Many Ghosts! a try.

—description from the publisher

エクスフルーツ (Ekusu Furu-tsu) EXLUSIVE FRUIT is a card game with a theme of exploration for fruits.

A rumor says that there are three islands in the distant ocean, where secret fruits grow. You try to collect fruits in these islands, but only one can enter each of these islands at a time.

Each player has four cards in their hand: three fruit cards (Red, Green, Blue) and pass.

Each round, three victory point cards are placed on the table face-up, attached to each island. Then players simultaneously play one card from their hand. For each island, if only one player played fruit card of that color, that player gain the victory point card attached to the island. If more than one player played the same island, no players gain the victory point card. If any player plays pass, that player gains two points.

After the sixth round, each player adds up their victory points, and conditional victory points awarded. The highest scorer wins the game.

Baladãna is a new and exciting strategy game for two players.
There are some obvious similarities with chess, however Baladãna is more abstract and even more complex and dynamic.
The biggest difference is that while in chess the way each playing piece can move is always the same, in Baladãna, the way figures can move is dynamic, determined by their 'powers'. The configuration of these powers, represented by the colored rings, is updated throughout the game - players can change the position of one ring as part of each move, placing up to 3 rings on any figure. When more rings of different colors are placed on a figure the powers combine.
The objective is to capture both of your opponent's golden rings.

—description from the designer

キャリーピース (Kyari- Pi-su) CARRYPIECE is a game for children, in which you learn one-digit addition and carrying.

Each turn, you draw two cards from the deck and add up these numbers. Advance your pawn a number of spots equal to the ones digit of the total. You win the game if your pawn landed on the goal spot. You lose the game of your pawn passed through the goal spot.

This game has an advanced game mode.

Rules are simple enough for children, but adults can enjoy the game. Suitable for your children's first board game.

キャリーピースは１ケタの足し算と繰り上げを学びながら遊べる、低年齢向け知育ゲームです。

publisher's summary

八人の魔術師 (Hachi-nin no majutsu-shi) Eight Wizards is a two player fantasy card game from Japan.

In the distant past, two rival beings possessing unimaginable magical power clashed for control of the world. In the end, their conflict triggered an incredible explosion of energy, resulting in the mutual destruction of these god-like beings. This explosion was so powerful, it twisted the very fabric of reality in upon itself, ripping the world in two and leaving time to flow in an infinite loop. Those who survived the brutal conflict soon realized the energy of these dead gods was slowly being drawn to the central point where the two worlds now met. Skilled in the art of energy manipulation, those known as Summoners are now able to harness the power stored in this central point, referred to as The Node, to conjure Wizards and Monsters to do battle. By defeating a conjured being in battle, Summoners can capture energy in special Gemstones. Only through the use of these energy-filled Gemstones can the loop finally be broken and order restored to the world. However, there is a catch. Only the side with the greatest amount of stored energy will survive when the two halves of the world merge once again. With the fate of their world at stake, the battle between the Summoners thus began.

A game consists of 4 rounds with 5 battles. Each round is played as follows:


     The Attacker chooses one card from their hand and plays it face-up on the table. Activate any abilities that apply.
     The Defender then chooses 1 card from their hand and plays it face-up in front of the Attacker's card. Activate any abilities that apply.
     After all relevant abilities have been applied, compare the cards' Power levels. The card with the higher value wins.
     Turn the losing card sideways to the defeated position. (In the case of a tie, turn both cards sideways.) Note: A card is not considered "defeated" if the battle ended in a tie.
     Assign the role of Attacker to the player who won the battle, and Defender to their opponent. (In the case of a tie, the roles of Attacker and Defender remain the same as the previous battle.)
     Repeat steps 1-5 until all 5 battles have been played OR the special win condition is met.


After all 4 rounds are finished, the player with more points showing on their Gemstones is declared the winner.

Scrapyard Rollbot is a solitaire roll & write game based on searching the scrapyard for parts to buid the best robot for the science fair. There are only so many places to search, and your nemesis, Rick, is searching the same yard! Set collection meets area control in this gem!

That’s only the first phase! During the second phase you get to add awesome bling to make your robot stand out even more! Who can make the best robot with scrappy parts? Spoiler alert: Rick has his sights set on that blue ribbon! Good Luck!

In Little Island Gift Shops, each player assumes the role of a shop owner which sells ocean treasures to the passer by, but is also part of the local community trying to thrive. In this cooperative game, players dive for goods and try to put the best possible combinations of goods in all shops, to get the best score possible for everyone. The lowest scoring shop determines the score for the group. Little Island Gift Shops is a set collection game at its core.

On your turn:

Decide how many hours you will dive,
Roll the corresponding dice: the longer you take, the more dice you roll;
Determine which goods you find,
Then either pay the goods as tribute to the ocean to gain powers, put them in your shop for sale, or gift them to your fellow shop owners.
To be successful in Little Island, you will have to both chase your own high score, but also care for the others at the table.

Little Island Gift Shops requires a four-sided die, a six-sided die, an eight-sided die, and a ten-sided die. Components include one player sheet per player, and one communal player aide.

—description from the publisher

In Coral Relief, seed and grow coral, attract diverse species to your reef for bonuses and abilities, and control outbreaks of the destructive Crown-of-Thorns Starfish! This is a pattern building game with the added bonus of being able to grow your pattern!

On your turn:

Roll the dice for everyone to use: and decide how the dice are used in your reef:
Two dice develop your reef; die results of 1-4 seed or grow coral to the next stage and results of 5-6 open up special powers.
The final die determines where the crown-of-thorns starfish strikes.
Seems pretty simple right? Not so fast! To attract different species with their own powers, you need to build patterns of coral. Will you be able to attract the right species for the powers you want, will you gain the rare diversity bonus, or will the crown-of-thorns starfish get the best of your reef?

Coral Relief requires three standard six-sided dice in three different colors. Components include one player sheet per player, and one shared diversity sheet per game.

description:
Vamos ao quadro (Let’s go to the black board) is a game for two to four players acting as student.
The goal is to complete the challenges on the books to get exam cards. Each exam card provides the player with points. Whoever has the most points at the end of the game, wins.
Using dice, players can collect the tiles needed to form words, numbers, signs, among other things, to complete the challenges on the books.
In each turn, there are three actions for each student: one mandatory and two optional. Before starting, students should look at their books and see what pieces they will need.

     “Go to the board” - Allocate dice and perform the dice actions. (mandatory)

     “Take a test” - Deliver all needed tiles to earn an exam card. (optional)

“Vamos ao quadro” é um jogo para dois a quatro jogadores.
O objetivo é completar os desafios dos livros para obter cartas de prova. Cada carta de prova atribui ao jogador pontos e quem tiver mais pontos ganha.
Através dos dados, os jogadores conseguem recolher as fichas que necessitam para formar palavras, números, sinais, entre outras coisas, de forma a completar os desafios dos livros.
Em cada turno, existem três ações para cada aluno, uma obrigatória e duas opcionais. Antes de começar os alunos devem observar os seus livros e ver que fichas é que irão precisar.

description:
Enter a fascinating fantasy world in which mystical tribes are torn apart by internal strife. Fierce power struggles and cunning strategies lead to epic confrontations deciding who has total supremacy in the Warring Lands.

Roots of Mali is a remarkable challenge for strategists as players compete against each other with characters represented by dice – despite zero luck being involved! By vanquishing an opposing creature, you gain might points equal to the creature's power level. The first player to collect 10 might points wins, and you score by tactically moving creatures and exercising their special powers to outwit your opponent, eliminating their character dice from the board, which represents the Warring Lands.

Roots of Mali is a expansion for the base game DiceWar - Light of Dragons. Each tribe consists of six different creatures with unique abilities and distinct power levels. Tribes from both editions can easily be distinguished by the color of their representative dice.

—description from the publisher

Note: The Roots of Mali stand-alone game can be found here.

Munchkin Pathfinder fans, expand your adventure possibilities with the 20 new Dungeon cards in Munchkin Pathfinder 3 – Odd Ventures! These Dungeons are based on the best-selling Adventure Paths for the Pathfinder Roleplaying Game from Paizo Inc. We've also delved into the game to find the most (in)famous gates, doorways and other passageways to use for the Portal cards. Your heroes will be going on some very Odd Ventures indeed!

—description from the publisher

When goblins aren't busy pillaging farms and slaughtering the defenseless... well, that's never. But if they did have downtime, they'd definitely play games with pretty dice like the ones in this pack. It also has four bonus cards for use with Munchkin Pathfinder or any Munchkin game.
Contains six 16mm dice and four Munchkin cards.

In this expansion for Schatten der Macht, we present four new factions (Krieger des Brazoragh, Korsmalbund, Clan von Nai Ashyrr, Mehrer der Macht) and their grey eminences. Added to this are 32 new organisations (including twelve fear-inducing demon cults), as well as cards and rules for mercenaries.

—description form the publisher (translated)

Staple, Gun is a 4-6 player free-for-all party card game.

In the far-off future of 2060, a super-powered crime lord Badass Harry rules the world with a tyrannical corporate grip.

Meanwhile back in 2020, an inept office worker Hapless Harry has just found himself in the midst of a battle between assassins, all who want to see him dead and take the future crime lord spot for themselves...

The goal of Staple, Gun is to find and kill Hapless Harry. Players draw and play 1 card each turn, from three different factions; Office who find Harry, Assassins who kill Harry, and Chaos who want to see the world burn

If player's fail to kill Hapless Harry before the deck runs out, then they all enter a final brawl to determine the winner and savior of the future.

—description from the publisher

Aventuria - Contintent of adventures and dangers. In Schatten der Macht, powerful grey eminences forge their plans, spies are send out to undermine organisations, adventurers are hired to complete dangerous challenges. 
In the opaque game of secret societies, no-one knows where the opponent will strike next, to bring his secret plans to fruition.

Graue Eminenzen is an expansion for Schatten der Macht. With the playing pieces and rules contained in this box, up to six factions can face each other at the same time, and battle for influence and power on the continent of Aventuria. Six new factions allow for more choice in grey eminences and special rules. Missions ensure that the game remains diverse and challenging after multiple plays.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Blitzkrieg! is a double expansion pack with 98 cards and 14 counters to reinforce your Tiger Leader game!

The Expansion includes:


     More Commander cards x60
     More Enemy Battalion cards x14
     More Special Condition cards x05
     More Mission Event cards x11
     More Objective cards x03
     More Campaign cards x05
     One two sided counter for each Battalion card x14

Panzers! is an expansion pack with 24 cards and 24 counters to reinforce your Tiger Leader game!

description:
'Nice Darts -CardGame of Darts- is a competitive card game themed on darts.

In the base game (Count-up), players use their hand cards to win three tricks, representing their three throws from the ockey.

One by one, each player plays a card from their hand, and each card has its rank. After all players play a card, the player who plays the card that has highest rank among them takes their played card back in front of themselves as a score card, and other played cards are discarded.

Once a player takes three score cards in front of themselves, that player finishes their play for this round, and waits for all players have finished.

When all players finished their play, they count up victory point according to their own score cards, and the player who has highest point wins!

There are three different mode for this game.
1. Count-up (カウントアップ)
Literally count up the scoring cards and the players compare their total points (as above).

2. Cricket (クリケット) (requires an expansion pack)
It plays as an area control game. Cricket for darts has variants but this card game supports 'All number Cricket'.
Team play is available for four players.

3. Zero-one (ゼロワン) (requires an expansion pack)
Count down from a number that ends '01', such as 301, 501, or 701. This game mode supports team play too.

ダーツをテーマにしたカードゲーム
ジャンルはトリックテイキングゲーム
ダーツが好きな人もトリックテイキングが好きな人も遊べる事を目指したゲームです。
基本ゲームでは、「CountUp」
拡張ボードを使用して「Cricket」と「x01」といったダーツを元にしたゲームも楽しめます。
publisher's summary
publishers: 758 Board Game Circle (758ボードゲーム会)

description:
Billing itself as a "Prehistorically Inaccurate Wargame", Caveman! pits miniature cavemen in savage combat against one another and an assortment of dinosaurs, terror birds, and UFOs.

Movement allowances are based on the players' hands (so the figures of players with larger hands move faster), and missile attacks are resolved by throwing things on the opposing player (not their figures).

—user summary

The rhyming charades game with the strange sounding name.

Inside the box you'll come across loads of weird and wonderful rhyming charades. Your job is to describe a rhyme, with your team trying to guess as many as they can in 30 seconds.

Steer to Glory is a resource management racing board game for 1-8 players simulating a Grand Prix around the racetrack. Holding your steering wheel you rely on the resources of your racecar or use your tactical cards and pilot abilities driving on the edge while your goal is certain: victory.

Grab your steering wheel as if you were jumping into your cockpit. Set up your own assembled racing car, choose a pilot whose abilities fits best your strategy, qualify with your tactical cards, or save them for the racestart and the race itself. Shift into 1st gear, avoid wheelspin and race for victory pushing the limits while using your pilot awereness and abilities along with tactical cards and your resources!

The Grand Prix takes place on one track of the two-sided gameboard. By literally shifting gears you gain movement points to spend during your turn. However, using the resources of your racecar - such as engine, fuel, tires etc. - you can gain extra speed. Along straights there's an opportunity to use slipstream or try to take over the car in front with a bold move from a certain distance. There are strict rules for cornering (based on gears and car setup), but using your resources wisely you can violate them and get further. Resources can be gained back during a pit stop.

Rulesystem is created so, that players get to know more and more functions of the elements as they approach advanced level.

'AI' drivers lets you fill up the grid when playing with a small amount of players or solo.

‚Team orders’ game mod lets you control two cars between wich you have to share your initial income evenly or favorizing one of them for victory.

You can also compete against an AI driver on your friends’ side in ‚Co-operative’ game mod during a four-round championship relying on each others’ developments while racing for 1st.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Squadron Commander: Small Naval Actions 1700-1830 – 2nd Edition
description:
Squadron Commander' is designed specifically to play with the 10mm Crew miniatures from Capitan Games, and the Gunboats & Ships made by Laser Dream Works for Capitan Games.

The main elements of the game are the gunboats and ships, along with their crews.

Gunboat, our original Napoleonic small naval action rule set, has grown and, with this second edition, has become Squadron Commander in an updated form. The game has been re-scaled for a better playability and is supported by a whole range of miniature crews and ships that are available in 10mm scale.

With this re-scaling, players will be able to play on a smaller game table with smaller gunboats or on, a larger table, with cutters, brigs of war, sloops, corvettes, frigates, Xebecs, and much more.  Also in this 2nd edition we have introduced two new kits from the great “Hearts of Oak” Ships of the Line a 4th rate (50 & 64 gun) and a 3rd rate (74 & 80 gun).

We are investigating the introduction of 2nd and 1st rate vessels later, together with a merchant or “Indiaman” vessel. As a result of these moves, we have made a small adjustment to the rules.

With these changes, the rules have been expanded and cover all ship types and there is a full set of ship characteristics and crew charts ready to play straight from the book. By including the great “Ships of the Line” and  Frigates, players will be able to recreate the famous frigate actions of the Napoleonic wars, with Privateers or take the role of a captain of the Royal Navy, like Lord Cochrane on HMS Imperieuse, the French Privateer, Surcouf, with Le Confiance, Stephen Decatur who fought against the Barbary pirates or Spanish Captain Barcelo and his gunboats attacking Gibraltar… all this using a single rule set… action on the high seas, coastal landings, shore actions, cutting-out operations… plenty of enjoyment and history in one book…

As a tactical game, Gunboat – Squadron Commander is designed to be played with a few ships per side, but ships which provide a strong visual experience as well as quick exciting game play, Squadron Commander is a new concept in naval games, emphasising the individual action of ships and crews rather than those of fleets.

—description from publisher

boardgame: Divisional Commander: Tactical Napoleonic Wargame Rules
description:
The huge influence of Napoleonic combat systems, tactics and weaponry (the flintlock musket was the standard weapon for all Armies until the later 1840’s), meant that they remained the standard throughout the world until the full development of the rifled musket. This weapon, which used percussion caps rather than a spark from a flint, together with rifled artillery changed tactics and formations, and ended the Napoleonic Era.

Divisional Commander (DC) is a set of Napoleonic rules to cover the whole Napoleonic era, including the British-American War of 1812, the “forgotten” Wars of Liberation in South America, and the last War of the Napoleonic Era - the 1st Carlist War.

The rules are designed to play battles of this period of medium size, involving a division, or a small corps. DC is designed to allow players to represent historical battles or to play balanced games based on a point system that allows them to play fictional scenarios and to run campaigns or championships.

To represent the intense movements and counter movements of the formations in Napoleonic battles,  DC (as other Capitan Games rules) uses a system that allows more flexibility in the game rather than an “I go, you go” system.

With the order system and the obligation to choose between different options for units, DC represents the fog of war and the uncertain control a commander has over his troops and his subordinate generals.

Included: Rules, Cover & Back Cover in High Resolution, Tokens & QRS, Napoleonic Army Lists, 1st Carlist War  Rules & Carlist Army Lists

—description from the publisher
family: History: Latin American Wars of Independence

boardgame: Company Commander: Tactical Napoleonic Rules Company & Squadron Level Actions
description:
Company Commander is set in the XVIII & the first half of the XIX century.  During this period there were many great battles with large armies and thousands of men but there were also many smaller actions where the use of a musket, ability in fencing, riding skills, personal courage and charisma were decisive.

Company Commander rules are designed for games between small units of men such as reconnaissance patrols, convoy escorts, intelligence missions, border attacks, and other missions which were given to small groups of selected troops. The actions of small groups of soldiers and the direct commands of their officers are the most important aspects of play.  The system simulates the frantic rhythm of an encounter.  The “human factors” of individual initiative, speed and leadership are worth more than the grand tactics of larger battles.

The rules are designed to use with 15/18mm figures. Figures can be based individually or in groups. But other scales as 28mm could be used.

For command figures, standard bearers, musicians and officers, one figure equals one man. In all other cases one figure represents three soldiers. 8-12 figures represent a section and three sections represent a company.

Each game turn represents about 3 minutes of time.

Ground scale is 1:100.

During 6 years the game has been developed with the suggestions of many players, clubs and hours of gaming.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Trireme Commander: Ancient Naval Tactical Combat Wargame Rules
description:
Trireme Commander is a game of tactical naval combat, focused on the simulation of combat between small fleets of galleys, rather than great naval engagements.

The objective of the simulation is combat between a few galleys in situations such as combat patrol, merchant escort, counter-piracy actions, and pirate assaults, etc. A game is best played with two to four galleys per player.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marlburian Commander: Tactical Wargames Rules for the Marlburian Wars
description:
Marlburian Commander is an adaptation of Napoleonic rules Divisional Commander, the tactics of Marlborough era develop the Seven Years War tactics and later on the Napoleonic’s.

In this years the linear formations and rigidity of the units were the rule in the battlefield, but wars develop very quick the tactics and strategy, in this case the Spanish Succession War develop this linear tactics to more flexible formations, to gain in firepower and control.

The art of Command formations in the battle was developed too in this war from a rather informal chain of command to a more developed chain of command and staff as the war goes on.

Included Army list for all the Major Nations of the era.

—description from publisher
family: History: War of the Spanish Succession

description:
Branle-Bas De Combat: Miniature Combat rules for naval warfare in the Age of Sails.
1/1200 scale
from 1765 to 1815

Each player controls 6-8 ships

Sequence of play: 
1- Test - if needed
2- Movements
3- Fire
4- Contact between 2 ships

Rules in French

—user summary

boardgame: Unstable Unicorns: Steampunk Unicorn Promo Card
description:
Released in November 2019 at convention Booths for the 2020 year reward. Card is printed in the 2nd Edition style.

Card text:
Magical Unicorn card
When this card enters your Stable, you may bring an Upgrade card from your hand directly into your Stable.

Energize!

Life on the ship is boring. Going down to the planet is where it's at. Add these 30 cards to your Star Munchkin game for a real landing party. But watch out for uninvited guests!

—description from the publisher

Time for Your Checkup!

Being a Cultist is a lot harder than you might think. It's good to stop occasionally and make sure you're OK. Add these 30 cards to your Munchkin Cthulhu game for the ultimate test of your sanity!

—description from the publisher

"Jura" is the highland region - the oldest part of Poland. You can find there a lot of old medieval castles, and ruins, monadnocks, limestones, caves and mystery places full of legends and unexplained stories.

As a player you will travel by car, on big board, which represent the road map of the region, visiting the tourist attractions to collect victory points. 
On the beginning of the game each player draw 6 cards from the stack – those are your first goals. You have to plan how to get there, using the network of first-class, and secondary roads. For visiting each place you have got on your “Attraction Card” you receive a reward. There are two kinds of them: victory points (Fossils), and tourist badges, which can make a significant difference if you will be able to collect 5 of the same kind.

Players can supply and exchange the cards, by visiting dedicated places on the map (Tourist Information Points). 
You can find also the special cards like: “Scenic Flights”, “Road under construction”, “4x4” and “Tourist Guide” which may change the game’s tides and provide negative interaction.

There are a few variants of the game: from the easy and quick one for children, to much more demanding ones, in which teams, containing of two or three players, can compete, using their own tactics to win.
The dynamics and emotions are increasing during the game.

—description from the publisher

Flap Flap Duck is a 2-5 players co-op games, where all players has only one objective which is to bring Ducky home, however the route home is not a simple path as there are many obstacle along the way. This is where you and your team will have to work together to maneuver the obstacle to prevent Ducky from hitting it. Using only clues that you have given and the limited actions in your hand, do you have what it takes to bring Ducky home?

—description from the publisher

“Jumble Order Old Tales” is an extended set of “ Jumble Order ”.
The new sets “Dwarf” and “Old Kingdom” bring new developments to the game. You will also be able to play some selection rules. ◆ Expansion Set Overview Expansion sets “Dwarf” and “Old King” are card sets equivalent to Jumble cards (additional / advanced). By selecting a new card set, you will be able to play jumble orders with a different feeling of play.

For two thousand years; from 3000 BC to 1000 BC, warriors fought battles with weapons of bronze. This gleaming red-gold metal is an alloy of copper and tin. The warrior nobles rode into battle in chariots and were clad in bronze armour. Their followers gripped bronze thrusting spears, battle-axes and slashing swords. Battle was a dazzling sight as weapons and armour glinted in the sun amid the clash of bronze against bronze and the clatter of charging chariots. This was the heroic age of legend! When the sand and rubble is cleared away from the mighty ruins of the temples, tombs and strongholds of this time; the tales of these battles are revealed inscribed in stone and on the imperishable tablets of baked clay.

Around the ancient carvings of the warriors, which inspire the model soldiers depicted in this book, are the reports of their deeds on the battlefield. From what they tell us we can recreate battles for our model armies. In this book you will find many battle scenarios and skirmishes all based on real events. We begin with the Sumerians, rival city states. Then we see the rise and fall of the first great empire of the Akkadians. Ancient Egyptians fight to unite their nation and repel Hyksos invaders. The mighty Hammurabi takes on rivals to create the kingdom of Babylon. The chariot Empires of the Hittites and Mitanni clash and try and hold their own against the New Kingdom Pharaohs and the Mycenaeans. Egyptian and Canaanite chariots battle on the plain of Megiddo. Heroes fight duels upon the plain of Troy. Then Sea Peoples and Habiru tribes challenge the chariot lords as we approach the dramatic end of Bronze Age.

Age of Bronze contains 32 scenarios - in addition to the traditional Hail Caesar massed battles we introduce skirmish rules for smaller scale conflicts between warbands so often seen during this period.

—description from Publisher
family: History: Wars in the Ancient Near East

description:
Caliber .45 is a game about wild west, cowboys, caravans and trains robbery, skirmishes and betrayals, all for the sake of the cherished million. Caliber .45 serves great for party board gaming. It can be played against each other, or divided into teams.

The essence of the game

Each player receives a cowboy card, then the cowboys take turns arming themselves, collecting cards from a common deck with weapons. One of the players, realizing that he is ready to go into the business, notifies other players and they can decide either to join the raid or refuse. Cowboys-robbers draw out a card of their target - it can be a stagecoach, train, bank or other card that brings money - then they will need to get rid of security, by spending their weapons. After that cowboys begin to divide the loot, which most often leads to a shootout, the winner receives the whole jackpot. In the loot deck there are not only trains and stagecoaches, there are also indians, a sheriff and other misfortunes that can ruin a robbery, send robbers to prison, or even kill an entire gang at once.

The cowboy's task is to earn his million. The one whose extraction brought this money becomes the winner.

You can play the game alone against all or teams, teaming up in pairs, in which case you will need to collect a million for two.

Who is this game for?

The board game is suitable for parties, table feasts in canyons and deserts, behind the bar of the saloon or family evenings on the farm. You can give it to children and adults, especially suitable for those who like simple games for the company, cowboy themes and spaghetti westerns, competitive card fillers, mustangs and armadillos.

—description from the publisher
Set of 3 promos cards (plus title card) distributed at Essen Spiel 2019. All 3 cards are new head-to-head trials: Instinct, Memory, and Agility.

boardgame: Hellenica: Story of Greece – Themed AI Deck expansion
description:
The Themed AI Deck expansion for Hellenica: Story of Greece expands the solo and AI playability of Hellenica by adding AI decks unique to each city-state. In the basic game, all city-states use the same AI deck.

Using the Themed AI DEck expansion, each city-state will be more likely to follow its historical path. Thus, Sparta will tend to focus on land power, Athens will tend to want to control the seas, etc.

Expand your Hellenica: Story of Greece experience using the Themed AI Deck expansion.

boardgame: Hellenica: Story of Greece – Leaders and Legends
description:
An expansion for Hellenica: Story of Greece that adds unique leaders to the game allowing players to modify the characteristics of their city-state.

Each leader confers a special bonus on the city-state that recruits them. For example, Heracles aids in fighting Mythological Creatures while Socrates makes your research more productive.

Each leader is represented by a deck of cards that appear in a market that players review to recruit the leaders that they want. But, watch out, other players may be desiring the same leaders as you and leaders will not serve two city-states!

—description from the publisher

The fun word game for quick thinkers, this time with chain reactions that catch you unawares! Try to continue the word chain, and find a word that matches an available category as fast as you can. Easier said than done - the word must begin with the final letter of the previous word. The ticking bomb sets the pace.

—description from the publisher

The year is 1935, and, taking a cue from Albert Einstein, Erwin Schrödinger has published a thought experiment known as “Schrödinger’s Cat.” A financial benefactor with an interest in physics and a lack of understanding of the term “thought experiment” has funded a special think tank to determine once and for all if the cat is in fact dead or alive. Regrettably, for those tasked with running the experiment, Schrödinger himself has snuck into the group and wishes nothing more than to sabotage the experiment as he believes the Benefactor has missed the entire point.

Schrödinger's Cat is a quick social deduction party game for 6-12 players. Game-play typically lasts 10-15 minutes. Player roles include a host, Lab Techs, Schrödinger himself, a Financial Benefactor and several Think Tank Physicists.

Roles are split into two teams, blue and red. On the blue team are the Financial Benefactor, the Honest Lab Tech and the Think Tank Physicists. Their goal is to determine weather the cat is dead or alive successfully. On the red team are Schrödinger and his Lab Tech. Their goal is to ruin the experiment.

The experiment is prepared by placing the two sided cat meeple into the experiment box (mint tin). One side shows the cat as dead, the other alive. The tin is passed and shaken by each player. The host reveals the status of the cat to the two lab techs. The host also introduces Schrödinger to his lab tech. The identities of Schrödinger and the lab techs are unknown to the other players.

Through stages of voting and deliberation the players determine if the cat is dead or alive. They also distribute influence to select a think tank chair. If think tank chair is on the blue team AND the think tank gets the state of the cat right, the blue team wins. If the red team tricks the think tank into voting wrong OR is selected as the think tank chair they ruin the experiment and win.

—description from the publisher

WARNING: Don't Get Drunk is not for the weak hearted! Don't Get Drunk is the ultimate party game that's bound to make anyone who plays it laugh out loud and have a night full of fun. Don't Get Drunk is the party game you've been waiting for, with 4 separate types of cards in the game, mini games, unique spots, and short / long cuts you're definitely going to have a crazy fun night!

The 4 card types are Luck, Quiz, Dare, and Trial, these unique cards will test your wits, bravery, and skills. You may be dared to ding dong ditch your neighbor, dance for a minute, post something wild on social media, or you might have to face another team in a game of pong, flip cup, or kings cup. Make sure to build your team accordingly!

HOW TO WIN AND PLAY: Be the first person or team to make it from the bar spot to the home spot. Roll the dice and move that amount of spaces, pick the corresponding card and do what it says, or don't, but beware there will be punishments! Don't be afraid to sabotage your opponents to get ahead in the game!

—description from the designer

Welcome to Facility 07 - the Tabletop-lite Game of Exploration and Espionage with a Battle Royale Twist. It’ll keep you laughing even as your meticulously planned attack is countered by another player and you end up in prison!

Play with 2-6 players as you become iconic spies and action heroes on a mission to infiltrate the evil genius Professor Norwood’s lair and save the world! Each game is unique as the facility is built out of cards room by room, offering over 1 million possible layouts. Find and equip powerful gadgets, stock up on action cards, and keep your covert cards secret in plain sight.

Inspired by spy genre parodies such as Austin Powers, Archer, and Venture Bros., Facility 07 is a light-hearted party game with a perfect balance of strategy and luck. Our game is approachable and friendly, designed to be easy to learn and teach to non-gamers. You'll have a blast exploring the facility and thwarting each others' plans!

Facility 07 is a tabletop-lite experience that merges the intrigue of a spy adventure with the engaging elements of a tabletop RPG. It’s perfect for players looking for a memorable experience at a relaxed pace.

In the game, players have an Agent card with Speed, Strength, Insight, and Finesse stats. They roll six-sided dice against their Stats to attempt exploration and combat challenges. Find and equip powerful and unique gadget cards, and keep a hidden covert card in play to get an edge over your fellow Agents.

Each Agent card features a unique ability and a humorous blurb of flavor text that corresponds to the character. The bold art style and engaging characters make it fun and easy for players to slip into character, perhaps to deliver a timely quote!

Drawing on years of experience running Tabletop RPGs like D&D and creating various game systems, we’ve worked to capture the essence of a TTRPG session in Facility 07, providing the fun and feel without the need for a game master.

—description from the designer

This card replaces another one from the Hydra deck. This card has no effect, you only gain victory points.

During the setup, if you are playing with the Hydra cards, replace one of the Hydra cards with the promotional card in question.
The cards will be placed in the pile in ascending order:
Hydra > Harpies > Chimera > Mother of monsters

The different promo cards can be added in individually.

This card was included in the first printing of the game.

This is the latest set of wargaming rules from Hordes of Models and Buckets of Dice. The rules facilitate phalanx, warband, company to regimental level games from 3000BC to 1901AD with a focus on command and control. Larger games can be played depending of the scale and size of the table available. The design philosophy of the rule set provides a simple approach that can be used to play wargame battles, irrespective of the figure scale (6mm, 10mm, 15mm, 25mm and even 1/32 scale). These rules have been designed to provide the player with challenges based around the manoeuvre and the management of their army in an easy format while still maintaining a feel for the specific period being played. The rules use a number of bases, depending on the size of formations. These are then grouped into distinct command groups. A formation has between two, four or six bases to represent the different formation sizes. The bases are placed side by side to represent a line, skirmish line or a column/block deployment. A base can have any number of figures on it according to the players preference or current basing arrangement. To represent scouting or skirmish formations, the formation bases are spaced out in a line, with each base having a gap equal to one base width. I personally, use blank bases to show clear formation definition. Artillery formations are based slightly different. They are always on a single square base of two base widths wide to two base widths deep, always counting as a standard size formation. When playing with scales of 6mm – 28mm, a base size of 60mm x 60mm is used, although the equally popular 40mm wide bases could be used instead. For a scale greater than this, bases of 100mm x 100mm are used. This is the base size I use for my 54mm games. The rules use a measurement of one base width equal to one range/movement distance measurement. The rules have been designed to provide capability options for each base. These bases are then grouped into formations. Each formation cannot complete all of the traditional actions/phases (shooting, moving, charging and reforming etc.) during a turn, with a specific formation. Each formation has two action points per turn. The players then decide which combination of actions to complete. The players complete all actions on a specific formation alternatively. This makes the style of play more of a conversion, keeping players engaged through the whole game. The framework used in the game looks at the different formations capabilities compared to that of compatible enemy formations in period. Or put more simply: “Was the specific formation any better or worse on the battlefield compared to their opponents, for the period?”. This enables the player to apply their own knowledge of the historic period to represent the way they believe a specific type of formation would have performed.

—description from publisher
