boardgame: Gunpowder Period: Quick Play Wargames Rules 1560AD - 1885AD
description:
Gunpowder Period: Quick play wargames rules for 1560AD - 1885AD

The design philosophy of the Gunpowder rules provides a simple approach that can be used to play wargame battles at brigade or higher command levels, irrespective of the figure scale (6mm, 10mm, 15mm, 25mm and even 1/32 scale) with your own current models and common basing formats currently in use. There are many very good rule sets aimed at a specific period in the hobby which provide a detailed simulation of warfare in that period. These rules have been designed to provide the player with challenges based around the manoeuvre and the management of their units in a quick play format while still maintaining a feel for the period. An army within the game is typically 20 units as side broken down into distinct command groups. A unit is a single base. The rules use 100mm equal to one range/movement unit and a unit has a footprint of 120mm wide by 30mm – 80mm depth depending the formation and unit type. The rules have been designed to provide capability options at the unit level, while still presenting the dilemma to the player that they cannot complete all the traditional phases (shooting, moving, charging and reforming) during a turn with that specific unit. These rules instead of using a traditional system of very distinct detailed weapon factors and micro-managed movement rates, they provide a game framework that looks at the different unit’s capabilities compared to that of the unit’s opponents for the period. This enables the player to apply their own knowledge of the historic period to represent the way a specific unit should perform with in the game. The rules also do not use the IGUGO turn approach, but instead each action, (reform, charge, move and shoot) can be done in any sequence and up to twice in a turn. This keeps both players engaged throughout a turn sequence. A unit has a limit of two actions in any turn. The players take it in turns to complete these actions by unit. Also, the rules do not focus on detailed weapon factors and actions within a specific game turn time frame, but use the approach: “Was the specific unit any better or worse on the battlefield for the period?” For example, all units could have used volley fire, but was this specific unit any better/worse at than anyone else? The rules manage this through a unit being given a selection of capabilities. The rules make a difference between the plug and socket bayonets. This is handled by the unit completing a distinct action to counting them fitted. This means when a plug bayonet is fitted, the unit cannot shoot. This formation is called “Foot cavalry defence”. This also covers the use the of foot squares as an anti-cavalry defence. The rules have to make a comprise and when a unit is claiming “foot cavalry defence” they also count as a deep formation even when a unit is in line with bayonets fitted. All units break at the same point, regardless of if the unit is veteran, regular or militia. What is managed within the rule set is how quickly or slowly a unit reaches that point of failure. Generals are used for command and control only. Units must be organised into command groups. An army for the game is organised into a number of these command groups with an overall army commander. Generals can only command units within their group and the army commander can command any units across all command groups. Typically, a command group is a brigade, division or corps depending on the scale you have adopted for the army. Games can be played over a two-hour time limit with a scoring system based around the number of units that one side has managed to kill. The rules reward aggressive play; thus, hopefully avoiding a static game. For a specific historical battle use the objectives of the actual battle to decide the winner. These rules cover the period from the end of the dominance of the Swiss Keil to the end of The Mahdist Wars in the Sudan.

—description from publisher

Infamously known as a concrete jungle, Hong Kong is a lot more than mere concrete as its reputation suggests. With about 70% of its 1,100 km2 area being vegetated, Hong Kong actually has more green than grey. Amongst the greenery are over 3,300 species of vascular plants, within which are more than 60 species of mammals; more than 500 species of birds; around 80 species of reptiles; some 20 species of amphibians and tens of thousands of different invertebrate species. These animals strive to survive every day. To them, Hong Kong is a dangerous place.

To survive, they will need to be able to find food and suitable habitats, avoid predation, outcompete other competitors and be able to adapt to or be lucky enough to avoid a myriad of human disturbances. If you are now part of this community, will you be able to master the Law of the Forest and survive in this harsh environment?

—description from the publisher

Super Nova Smash! is a fast-paced phone + card party game for 2-8 players about racing through the blast of a supernova. The goal is to get rid of your cards before your friends — and do as much damage as you can while you go.

The deck consists of 96 cards, numbered 0-5. You can play one card higher or lower than the card on top of a play pile, including your on your own pile, and those numbers wrap, so you can play a 5 on a 0 and vice versa. The "0" card is special, though, in that it counts as damage (negative points), counted at the end of the round, so it's best to try to play them on other people's piles while defensively keeping your pile as far from 0 as you can.

The app is there to tell you when to play, give you random challenges, and generally make your life a panic-inducing nightmare (in a fun way!). The app will randomly (but evenly) assign turn order and occasionally force all players to compete in quick, frantic mini-games on the device — often when they least expect it.

The first person to get rid of all their cards and tap the button in the middle of their screen gets the most Space Points, with fewer points being given to each player as they finish. Once all players are done, they are asked to count their damage (cards marked "0"), and then the app will calculate their scores for the round.

Rounds typically last about 3-6 minutes, and the player with the most Space Points at the end of 10 rounds wins!

—description from the designer

Master the Elements uses a square, 8” by 8” board, 24 double-sided wooden Element tokens, and 10 colorful Element cards. It also includes a custom “Master the Elements” scoring pad.

Players start by each drawing a secret Element card – whichever element is represented on this card is the element for which that player can collect points at the end of the round. The board starts out empty, with all Element tokens mixed up and spread out around the edges of the board.

Players take turns stacking and stealing element tokens and piles to shift the balance of the elements in their favor. The top element on any pile must match the corner of the board in which the pile is placed, however, as players stack Master tokens and strategically combine piles, even the element with the fewest tokens at the beginning of a round can end up with the largest piles at the end.

Alliances are tentatively made and broken as secret element cards are revealed and concealed, and chaos battles with strategy as each player tries to become the Master of the Elements.

Once the last token is placed, all game-play freezes and the round ends. Players tally up their scores (including bonus points for placing the last token) and reshuffle the cards and tokens to start again! Whoever gains the most points at the end of 4 rounds of play is crowned the winner and Master of the Elements!

—description from the designer

New and dangerous Fiends have appeared, and now make every dungeon even more of a challenge to conquer! In this expansion to One Deck Dungeon, new Fiend cards will harass you from the moment you step foot on the first floor right up until you face the boss. Each fiend has its own unique effects, and you'll have to divert some of your valuable dice to combat its threats during every encounter. It's not all bad news though - every time you push back the fiend, you'll gain a valuable point of healing and a heroic die.

One Deck Dungeon: Abyssal Depths contains 6 large Fiend cards, and 3 Heroes - the Dragoon, Aquamancer, and the Witch.

—description from the publisher

After centuries of machinations, Hades finally defeated the other gods. He is about to drag mankind into the underworld to reign supreme. Only the wise old Daedalus, inventor of genius, still stands against him, helped by a handful of heroes. But these will not cooperate: only one will be the savior of humanity.

Hades Trap is played on a modular board made up of 9 tiles, which allows you to renew your games. Each player plays as a hero who will be able to perform a variable number of actions in turn. You will be able to move around or interact with the square on which you are standing, in order to fight skeletons, activate mechanisms or even knock out your opponents! But beware, if you step on a breadcrumb trail, the Minotaur will be alerted and will run in the direction of its patrol! Maybe this is your chance to pull it towards your enemies?

Collect three of the available medallions and exit the labyrinth to be declared the winner. The medallions can be retrieved in many ways, so you'll need to be resourceful and brave to complete this important quest.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Don’t just the thought of forest fires scare you?

Most wildfires are caused by careless humans and putting them out is hard. Imagine what happens when a mischief-maker like Kirik Veera knowingly sets fires to forests? Enter Kutty and his team of forest Rangers, out there to save the forest and its wildlife. They will do all it takes to put out those fires before they cause much harm. They are also always keeping an eye on Kirik, trying to put an end to his mean ways by sending him behind bars.

Night Fire is a battle of wits between Kirik and Kutty’s team. Kirik knows the forest inside out and how to make off in an instant. Kutty’s team is ever ready with their copters, fire trucks, jeeps and other gizmos to fight fires. And this means we have plenty of action in waiting!

This game is a tribute to these brave men who guard and protect our forests. It has been designed incorporating various elements of this cat and mouse game between the Rangers and the Brigands

A board game for four players, Night Fire will make you think, plan and act with lightning reflexes. It calls for excellent teamwork from the side playing Rangers and high survival skills for the player who stands in for Kirik. A game loaded with high drama and suspense till the end, right from the word go. Win or lose, fun and excitement guaranteed!

—description from the publisher

Cerberus – expansion for Crime Writers game

Cerberus is a fictive ‘true crime publication’.
Each card includes an underlined fact presented in the form of a crime-related article’s blurb.
You can score extra points by incorporating this fact into your Crime Writers story…

—description from the publisher (English)

A Cerberus egy “fiktív” bűnügyi újság. Minden Cerberus kártyán az újság egy oldalát látjuk, rajta pedig egy bűnügyi vezércikk kiemelését egy vastag keretben, mely mindig egy-egy kulcsfontosságú elemet ad hozzá a húzott alapkártyákhoz.
Eme fontos elemet a játékosoknak szintén be kell építeniük a történetükbe úgy, hogy az általuk felvázolt bűntény következményeként kialakult nyomozásról szóló cikk állítása igaz legyen…

—description from the publisher (Hungarian)

Be the first to fill your brain with brain cells in this game for all the family!
In this quirky quiz each card refers to a famous landmark. There are three questions per card worth either 1, 2 or 3 brain cells. You choose the level of difficulty and the correct answer fills your brain card with either 1, 2 or 3 brain cells.
The first to fill their brain card is the winner!

boardgame: つみきの王子さま (Tsumiki no Oujisama)
description:
In つみきの王子さま ("Tsumiki no Oujisama", "The Prince of Toy Blocks"), you have to build a castle out of tiles featuring toy blocks along with roofs, castle residents, bridges, and various kinds of windows. The tiles are all 2x3 but their contents vary wildly, and placement rules just require that every block be supported. The ultimate shape of your castle is up to you!

There are four main ways to earn points: you can have the highest or second highest roof in each of three colors, you can build a bridge that spans two tiles, you can place a castle resident at their preferred height, and you can add a new shape of windows to your castle. These goals interact in interesting ways - for example, to get roof points you want a very high castle, but most residents prefer relatively low levels of the castle.

Normally you place one tile each round, but if you place a colored toy block next to another colored toy block you get to place one extra tile on your turn. This can be used to take tiles from the shared stock in quick succession or to make a surprise play.

Once someone has played their 15th tile, play continues until everyone has had the same number of turns before entering final scoring. The game is flexible and can be played as a casual game with children and families, but the scoring and high degree of freedom leave plenty of room for serious play. What kind of castle will you make?

—user summary
publishers: イマジンゲームズ (imagine GAMES)

boardgame: Munchkin Collectible Card Game: Alpaca Wanderer Promo Card
description: This foiled promo Hero card was offered as a reward for certain tiers of the Dized Kickstarter campaign in December 2018.

The Madoushi miniature is a substitute option for any Ninja at the start of the PvP Dawn Escape game. It was originally offered as a reward for certain backers of the Dized Kickstarter campaign in December 2018.
 Madoushi has disguised himself as a Ninja in the Midnight Raid and now shows his true self to use his magi and steal the glory.

House Rules – who gets to play him (First or last Ninja on the Roof in the Midnight Raid etc.):
Simply replace your Ninja Miniature with the Madoushi miniature and swap your 3 Wana Tokens with the 3 Magic Cards.
Like the Wana Tokens, the Magic cards are single use and each should be discarded once used.

Magic Cards:

     Yadoku (Poison Dart): Causes target to double up in pain and fall back to their last location.
     Makaze (Evil Wind): Opponent is lifted high into the air and moved by the Madoushi two tiles in any direction.
     Kyūketsu (Blood Leech): A vile flying leech conjured by the Madoushi will always find its target, even if in cover or not in line of sight. Effect: Knock-back (1 tile)


—description from the publisher

FiveLines is a game played while listening to music, and the active player must play a card according to the rhythm of that music. (Soundtracks are available on the publisher's website.)

Your goal is to co-operate to achieve a high score. On the beat, you play one of your four cards in hand to the end of the line, then draw a new card. If you miss playing on the beat, you'll play one fewer card total and probably miss out on points. Each card has five lines on it, so when placed together, they look like a music score. Sometimes a line is blank, sometimes it has 1-2 colored symbols on it, sometimes it has an X over the colored symbol, and sometimes the symbol has a scoring halo around it.

Once the music ends, you advance from the first card onward with a pawn, stopping whenever you hit a scoring halo. You then count backwards to see how many symbols of that color (shape) there are before you hit an X, with the symbol inside the halo also counting. Sometimes a halo has an "x2" mark to indicate that you double the score for that halo. Some cards consist of nothing more than a "cancel" symbol, which tells you to ignore Xs on either side of that card. Combine your cards to score lots of points, with anything higher than 100 being excellent!

Players start in a cell, deep in the Deathwatchers dungeon. Their objective is to navigate the dungeon and it’s traps to escape. To prevent this, the Deathwatcher sends his curse to hunt the players, killing them on contact. If a player should die, he transforms into a minion of the Deathwatcher. This player can still win by scoring points when he kills other players. Players use standees to move on the gameboard and a charactersheet to keep track of their scores and possessions.

The dungeon is represented by a non-modular map which has several routes of varying length with unexplored rooms that are only revealed when a player enters them and are either a dead end or not. This ensures the need for flexible planning by players. It is up to each player to plot a route through the crypt and modify their plan according to revealed rooms and the actions of other players.
In their turn players must first choose how they move. Either slowly and sneaky to attract less attention and find more treasure or fast and reckless to cover more ground. 
Then they roll the dungeon die to see what happens to him or her that round. This will be highly influenced by the choice of movement.
Lastly they must deal with obstacles like traps or combat which occurs when a player draws a monster card or when a player that became a servant of the Deathwatcher catches a still living player.

—description from the designer

Join Rainbow Ralph on this roller coaster ride of fun! Spin him around and around until he gets dizzy. With a bit of luck, he’ll get sick. If he “Ralphs” on your turn, collect the string. The first player to collect 3 piles of string wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Gone Flying is a game about flying. Although invented by a pilot, players need no prior knowledge of flying, only basic aviation terms will be used and each will be fully explained. Played individually or in pairs, Gone Flying combines skill and chance to provide hours of entertainment for family and friends.

The object of Gone Flying is the be the first pilot(s) to fly your airplane from Terminal A to Terminal B.

—from the back of the box

This new club token replaces at the beginning of the game one of the two decoys. The troll standing on the cave tile with this token revealed picks an additional apple.
This promo comes with the corrected version of two tiles.

This set of mini-expansions adds new tools and challenges to the base game. Each expansion can be played separately or in combination with any other.

The Fair Youth expansion adds an additional deduction task to the game: you must discover where in London the Fair Youth is hiding. Bystanders will give you a clue for which way the Youth went from your current location, but if you are already at his actual location you will be told a lie. Deduce his location to surprise him there, or for extra points catch him and the Dark Lady together, unmasking them both at the same time.

The Rendezvous challenges you to discover which of seven landscape features acted as a meeting place for Shakespeare and his Dark Lady. Querying a location gives you a number from 1 to 3, being the number of steps away from the current location that the Rendezvous symbol can be found. Deduce which map symbol represents the Rendezvous, then confront and unmask the Dark Lady in a location with that symbol at it.

The Rival Poet adds a second pawn that stalks the streets and interacts with the player via simple movement rules. Choose from two different poets to make the game easier or harder. The Elusive Poet tries to stay one step ahead of you, but if you catch him he will reveal a clue about the Dark Lady. In contrast, The Aggressive Poet hounds and chases you, potentially spoiling your plans to intercept the Dark Lady.

Waypoints and Traces gives you two tools to mark and track the Dark Lady's location at a particular point in the stealth deck. And The Darker Ladies replace the clue cards in the base game, giving each Dark Lady a new set of characteristics and presenting a slightly harder deduction challenge.

—description from the designer

The Ganja card game allows people to socialize while also learning about the biology and perils of farming weed (Cannabis sativa). Players have fun growing your garden and protecting it from aphids to male plants!

Each player starts 4 cards. Players take turns drawing one card from the deck and putting one card into play. Each play, you will have a choice or planting, or using an action card to slow down the progress of your competitors. The goal is to grow your garden by placing a female plant card in front of you, face up so that all can see. To close a round simply put down 6 female plant cards in your garden, the person who grows the most plants wins the game. If you want a longer game simply tally points over three rounds. Action cards are available to slow your competitors while teaching everyone about the biology and perils of farming weed.

      Male plants can be placed in a competitor's garden. If that player doesn't hold, draw or trade a "Gardener", then their whole crop will be lost.
      Kangaroos steal the largest plant card
      Deer eat/destroy the largest plant card
      Aphids destroy the smallest plant card each turn unless "Ladybugs" are used, or no cards remain
      Ladybug card eats one "Aphid" card
      Edibles can be given to another player, and they will skip a turn
      Raided: the legal limit is 4 weed plants, so give this card to a competitor and they will skip a turn, and loose all but 4 plants


—description from the designer

boardgame: The Peninsular War: Paper Soldiers for Wellington's War in Spain
description:
The Peninsular War - Paper Soldiers for Wellington's War in Spain.

The second title in The Paperboys on Campaign series, some 46 pages of artwork enabling you to make French, British, Portuguese, Spanish, and several other countries’ troops which fought for and against the Iron Duke in his epic campaign against Napoleon’s forces.

—description from the publisher

Rules are available on the publisher website

It seems that another element wants to join the action from a different Wells book, and that may provide some apocalyptic chaos.

—description from publisher

description: An expansion released with the third Kickstarter campaign that adds a fourth level to every hero from quests 1-9.

The game is a two player abstract and strategy game. the goal is to collect the most ring points by games end.  Players use their 5 ring-collecting agent pieces to obtain the precious rings from the board. Each ring has it's own value and corresponding score, silver is one point, gold is three points and luxury gold is five points. Players must collect more total ring points than their opponent to be the winner at the game's end.

Board: 5x5 or 5x7 grid board.
Pieces: Each player has own color 5 ring collecting agent pieces.
Rings: 1 luxury gold, 4 gold and 10 silver rings(5x5 grid board) or 20 silver rings(5x7 grid board)
Player: 2
Playing time: 10 to 20 minutes

- Playing
Decide the first player and take turns alternately. Players must move  any one of their own pieces on the board or replace one of their re-use pieces previously captured by their opponent. Re-placing piece must be placed on the space where a ring exists and becomes a ring holder agent as described below.  Players can't pass their turns when they have a legal move.

- Basic moves
Any agent pieces can move one space to any eight directions, vertically horizontally and diagonally like chess king except for a space where own piece exists.

- Game end
There are two game ending conditions. The first is after all rings on the board are picked up, both players get one last turn. The second is when both players pass consecutively.

- Winner
The player who obtains more ring points at the end of game, wins the game.

オムそばすけっと (Omusobasket) is a set of cards with ingredients on them that you combine to make food in four different games.

In the base game there's a card in the middle to start, and on your turn you play a card from your hand so that the middle card and your card can be used to make a food item. So if the middle is "tomato" you could play "bread" to make pizza, and then the next person could play "curry" to make nan and curry. You go on this way until someone runs out of cards. An important rule is that you can't just invent some kind of food on the spot, you have to use an existing well-known dish.

The other games use the same principle of making food out of ingredients. In "Cooking Poker" the hands are based on combinations of food, so for example a "straight" is where you use all your five cards to make a course meal and can explain the order the courses are served in. In "Indian Food Cooking Poker" you play with your hand visible only to the other players, and "Tanuki Soba" is like Old Maid.

What food will you come up with?

—user summary

Le Kriegspiel Napoléon is a miniatures simulation of combats during the French First Empire, at the Grand Tactical level.

Sequence of play is semi-alternate:
player A moves while player B can do limited reactions

Brigade / Division level
distance unit or hexagon = 150 m
turn= 20 min
Miniatures: 6 to 28mm
Compatible with other rules basing standards
Army list: 16 armies

Rippa has led his grumbling and mutinous companions to Beastgrave in pursuit of the magnificently shiny choppa he sees every night in his dreams. With that blade in his hand, surely Rippa could ride back to his tribe and claim rulership over the headless corpses of all his old rivals.

This warband expansion set allows you to add Rippa's Snarlfangs to your games of Warhammer Underworlds. A set of push fit models in coloured plastic allows you to get your warband built and ready for gaming in mere minutes, while fighter cards let you keep track of them in game. You’ll be able to harness the unique tactics of Rippa's Snarlfangs with 32 warband specific cards, including objectives, upgrades and ploys. Meanwhile, 28 Universal cards give you new options for customising your deck, whichever warband you play.

This set contains:
3 push fit Rippa's Snarlfangs miniatures
3 fighter cards – to represent each warrior in your games (Rippa Narkbad; Mean-Eye; Stabbit)
60 unique cards to enhance your fighters and provide new tactics and challenges for your games of Warhammer Underworlds: Beastgrave, including:

     12 Rippa's Snarlfangs objectives
     10 Rippa's Snarlfangs upgrades
     10 Rippa's Snarlfangs ploys
     9 Universal objectives
     10 Universal upgrades
     9 Universal ploys and spells


—description from the publisher

boardgame: It's a Wonderful World: Heritage Edition
description:
The Heritage edition contains the base game as well as additional content not found in the retail edition including  "War or Peace" Heritage Campaign, "Leisure & Decadence" Heritage Campaign, and Easter Egg cards.

About the game:
In It’s a Wonderful World, you are an expanding Empire and must choose your path to your future. You must develop faster and better than your competitors. You’ll carefully plan your expansion to develop your production power and rule over this new world.

It’s a Wonderful World is a cards drafting and engine building game from 1 to 5 players. Each round, players will draft 7 cards and then choose which ones will be recycled to immediately acquire Resources, and which ones will be kept for construction to produce Resources each round and/or gain victory points.

When a card is fully built, it’s added to the player’s Empire to increase the player’s production capacity for each round. The mechanical twist being that the production phase works in a specific order. You'll have to plan your constructions carefully!

In addition to the base game, players can also enjoy expansions boxes introducing an innovative Campaign mode. Each Campaign offers a storyline to follow and many gameplay twists. At the end of each campaign, players will open a reward booster to unlock new cards, enhance their base game and keep a memory of what happened during the campaign. All the campaigns can be replayed and don’t imply game components destruction.

boardgame: Warhammer Underworlds: Beastgrave – Champions of Dreadfane
description:
Two deadly warbands arrive in Warhammer Underworlds Beastgrave with one epic expansion! Champions of Dreadfane gives you the opportunity to add the spectral ghasts of Lady Harrow’s Mournflight and Ironsouls Condemnors, previously only available in the Warhammer Underworlds: Dreadfane set.

Lady Harrow’s Mournflight are a fast-moving, aggressive warband, capable of bypassing fighters and lethal terrain alike with ease and dealing devastating damage. If you're looking for a fast, flexible and ferocious warband, this is the one for you!

Ironsoul's Condemnors are durable, hard-hitting and highly elite, equally comfortable defending objectives and crushing enemies up-close. In Warhammer Underworlds, they're a nigh-unstoppable wall of sigmarite – if you're looking for a small warband that packs a punch, grab them for yourself.

This set contains:


     3 push fit Stormcast Eternals Sequitors (Ironsoul's Condemnors) in blue plastic
     3 fighter cards, including Inspired rules on the reverse
     20 lronsoul's Condemnors power cards
     12 Ironsoul's Condemnors objective cards



     4 push fit Nighthaunt Myrmourn Banshees (Lady Harrow's Mournflight) in bone-coloured plastic
     4 fighter cards, including Inspired rules on the reverse
     20 Lady Harrow's Mournflight power cards
     12 Lady Harrow's Mournflight objective cards


—description from the publisher

A mighty Dragon is raiding all the country and a knight is traveling from a far away lands for defeating the beast and letting those lands gorgeous as a bunch of roses, as they were before.

SANT JORDI. La llegenda is a 2 player game which recreate the Saint George Legend. One player will represent the Knight which is trying to travel from far away and face the Dragon. The other player will represent the Dragon which will try to discover why Saint George don't pass through certain territories. The game includes different degrees difficulties for playing with kids and also with more experienced players.

During the set up, the Knight will draw a condition card the will give him a condition under that will never pass through a certain kind territory.

Turn by turn the Saint George and the Dragon will choose a card among the 5 cards available. If the Knight can pass through the chosen cards will collect shields for passing through baronies, as soon as pass through 3 baronies will win the game.

On the other hand if Saint George can't pass through a territory the Dragon will remove a card of the current cards passed through and take blindly a token from the bag. If it is a villager he will remove the token from the game but if it's the princess/prince then will remove 5 cards from the deck or try to solve the condition card of Saint Jordi. In case of resolve the condition, the Dragon wins the game but if doesn't guess the right condition, Saint Geroge wins the game.

Finally, if the deck has run out, the Dragon wins the game.

—description from the publisher
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
In Numbermatch you need to make sums.
You need to start in with the 4 numbers in the middle of the board and from that way you can add, subtraction, multiply and divide the numbers to lay down the answer.

Every player starts with 7 numbers (randomly given at the start of the game).
Players go by turn and choose a number from there own board to lay down at a sum. The number that you lay down is the score that you make

Example: 6 6 36 because 6x6 is 36. You got 36 points..
The next player can lay down 42 if he has this number..6+36=42.. Or 30 etc..

In your turn you can also swap numbers from your board from randomly pickt numbers out of the bag.

When a player is out of numbers the game ends and all the scores (written down by a player) are added to see who has the most points.

A game to use for math leasons and to learn your math skills.

The Aesir are dead, and the Vanir, now uncontested in their divinity, come to claim what is left of the Nine Realms. The War Clans must oppose, or align with, these new foes.

' Ragnarok: The Vanir'  features new Godsparks that War Clans may harness to combat the marauding Vanir, as well as new scenarios, monsters, and options to further develop players' campaigns.

'—description from the publisher

boardgame: Judge Dredd: Helter Skelter – Dark Judges
description:
The barrier between worlds has shattered. Now, the Dark Judges have returned to exact judgement on Mega-City One. The city is guilty. The crime is life. The sentence is death.

Judge Dredd: Helter Skelter – The Dark Judges is an expansion for the base game that introduces The Dark Judges, a new team that can be played against others in Judge Dredd: Helter Skelter or competed against on your own in a solitaire game.

—description from the publisher

"Narvik 1940" is a wargame for two players that simulates the tactical combats around Narvik in May and June 1940 between the French (Mountain troops*, ski scouts and legionaries) and the Germans (mountain infantry, sailors from sunken destroyers, and paratroopers). The rule is an evolution of the system En Pointe Toujours (At the Cutting Edge). Several scenarios are presented, both on the French attacks and the terrible German counter-attacks.

Complexity : 6/10
Solo playability : 4/10
One turn = 10 minutes
One unit = 4 - 10 men

—description from the publisher

description:
Wildlands: The Ancients is a big box expansion for Wildlands, bringing solo and cooperative play to the critically-acclaimed game, alongside support for 5-6 players.

The Ancients, age-old guardians of the arcane crystals, have long slumbered in their secret vaults. Rumored to be invincible sentinels, they have defended their crystals unchallenged for eons. With the fall of the Empire, the most desperate bands of the Wildlands have joined forces to take them down and claim the crystals they protect.

boardgame: すべてが登米になる (Subete ga Tome ni Naru)
description:
すべてが登米になる (Subete ga Tome ni Naru, "Everything Will Become Tome") is an area control game where you are a surveyor deciding how to redistrict the city of Tome in Miyagi Prefecture. The area control aspect is fairly traditional with two major twists: areas will merge as you play, and there are unique rules for hand management.

Every player starts the game with the same hand of nine cards, one for each district in Tome, but the cards are shuffled and can't be reordered after picking them up. Each round you pick two of the first three cards in your hand and use one to determine where you place cubes and the other to determine how many cubes you place. Cards that let you place two cubes correspond to more central and thus valuable districts, so you have to choose between good placement or more cubes.

After a district is filled with cubes accounting takes place, and typically after each accounting a "friendship token" will be placed between two districts, connecting them. After that filling individual districts still triggers accounting, but during accounting cubes in directly connected friendly districts are counted the same as cubes in the district itself. In the extreme case you could even take first place in a district you have no cubes in if you've packed a friendly neighbor.

—user summary

description:
A tactical soccer game on a 21x17 board.

Soccer in a different way.

Two teams of 5 players (pawns) and a ball (cylinder). Markers for scoring, possession and hits.

Players play a soccer match, trying to score goals and defend during an agreed amount of time. Players have two actions per turn. An action can be either run/shortrun, hit or counthit.
Every square on the board is one meter.

Running can be in a maximum of 3 meters in every direction, changing direction 45  degrees once if desired. 
Hitting shoots the ball a maximum of 4 meters sideways of forward (diagonal or straight.
Counthit is passing for a maximum of 5 passes.

Intercepting the bal is also possible. So is dribbling.

No dice/luck involved, just thinking and planning.

手札のないトリテ (No Hand)
description:
In 手札のないトリテ (No Hand), a trick-taking game, rather than being dealt a hand of cards, a number of cards are flipped face up to the table and the players then draft which one they have played.

At the start of the game, each player is dealt a card which relates to the conditions for the current hand, such as diamonds are trump, or players do not need to follow suit.  Each player also then passes that card to their left.

Play then proceeds through 10 tricks with a dealer setting out cards one trick at a time, and the players marking with a colored cube which card they will have played.  Once the 10 tricks have been "played", the players bid on how many tricks they have won, and the condition cards are revealed.  Players will score points based upon the accuracy of their bid and which cards they had knowledge of at the beginning of the game.

Micro Martians is a pocket-sized tile drafting game about building a colony on Mars.

Players compete to provide the most benefit to the colony by building modules depicting a variety of resource symbols.

To start the game, 32 double-sided tiles are shaken up and scattered on the playing surface.

Each turn, a player may gather a resource, or spend resources to build one of the available modules.

When one player can no longer complete an action, the game ends, and players score based on how many kinds of symbols they collected, as well as collecting the most symbols of each kind.

The game officially supports 2-3 players, but is manageable with up to 5, and as a solitaire puzzle, wherein the player(s) try to place all of the modules before the resources become deadlocked.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Say What You See: Music Edition
description:
This music based quiz game is laugh-out-loud funny and easy to pick up and play in no time. Ideally suited to three or more players, the game can be played by anyone with a love for music! The game comes with 100 catchphrase cards, each with a different well-known artist, song, or musical phrase that the player must guess by deciphering the pictogram clues.

Each clue is a representation of a famous name or phrase, so all the guesser needs to do is say what they see for the answer to seem obvious. Pictogram or Rebus style games have been hugely popular for generations, using images, symbols, or clever wordplay to denote meaning that is often clearest when read aloud. With a retro style box designed like a classic cassette, Say What You See: Music Edition makes can be neatly stored away when not in use. Full instructions included.

—description from the publisher

In Houses Of Knowledge, players  take the roles of curators trying to build the most diverse and impressive museum possible. But beware, as museum curation is a cut throat world, and your competitors will not hesitate to steal items from your museum to place it in theirs, disrupt the market to get better prices, or use talented PRs to raise their revenue.

Houses Of Knowledge is a collection game tasking players with placing items in thematic rooms in their museums. The first player to reach eight or more items on display will trigger the end game condition, as long as items or action cards are still available. Items on display bring more revenue which is used to buy rooms, more items, but also action cards.
Action cards take the shape of different characters.Each of them offer different perks, such as protecting your museum, reorganising the market or stealing cards from your opponents' hand.

Houses Of Knowledge relies on the capacity of the players to use the different actions efficiently in potentially huge combinations of cards synergies during their turn, while trying to prevent other players to do the same.

Bold traveler! Colect tiles of bones on your desert board. Complete your character and, sing!. Nothing can stop your song.

My little pouch of bones is a boardgame proyect for children. A familiar game which also you can usein the classroom. It’s created specificaly to chids with the intention that they learn the body scheme singing.

If you already know us, you know that or games have a musical component that it´s not simply a soundtrack or a setting. And if you just find us, you know that we’ re going to make mischief with music.

In this case, we have created a song to remember some body parts.
In some moments, even, we name some parts of the human skeleton or bones.
We hope you like.

COMPONENTS
The game has:

     1 pouch
     4 boards of skeleton-character, reversible
     44 wilcards of skeleton-character.
     20 tokens breath-live
     12 wildcards of the desert


We tell you!!
Know or peculiar family, an arab father, a native mather, an European adopted child and an asian cousin who was orphaned.

SETUP

      Chouse a character which you’ll play.
      Take the board with your character.
      Every player do the same.
      Set aside the rest of boards.
      Put the tiles of the chosen character in the pouch of bones.
      Place the breath-life tokens on the table. It varies according to the number of players.
      The first player will be who’s the last one who make a trip.


GAME SECUENCE

     At firts of your turn take two tiles from the pouch.



     Check if one of them has a piece of your character.



     If it does, you can place it on your skeleton by the side that fit with the bones. If bouth tiles are from your character you can place them on your skeleton. In case that one of them is from other character you can place it on his skeleton if you have place on yours first, always by the bones side. In case that you took two tiles of other characters ends your turn and discart the tiles on your little pouch with skeleton side up.



     When you place a tile on the skeleton of other player put a breath-life token of your character color over the tile. In case that you take a tile of desert you can remove tokens of other players that are over your skeleton.



     Any player with a tile of desert can can take a tile of the little pouch of other players. If it’s of his character, he place it on his board for the bones side, if it’s of other player character he can place on the board of that character an a breath-life token of his color.


The game ends when the pouch get empty

SCORE

     Every tile place on your character for you score 2 points.
     Every tile place on the skeleton of other player score 1 point.
     Wins who has more points.


GAME MODE: sing to your skeleton

      After score, remove the breath-life tokens and take every tiles that you have on your skeleton
      Flip the board and play with your character side.
      Prepare the song “My little pouch of bouns” in your tablet, smartphone or computer.
      Who place the tiles for the side of his character on the board faster while the song is playing won 3 poins.

1 mini = 40-60 men
1 gun = 2-4 guns and crew
1 turn = 2-3 min real time

1- Setup - before the battle starts

2- Start of turn
2.A - Orders phase
2.B - Rally phase
2.C - Initiative
2.D - Activation and orders resolution for side A. B can do opportunity reactions.
2.D.I - Charges declarations
2.D.II - Charges moves
2.D.III - Regular moves
2.D.IV - Fire
2.D.V - Mêlées
2.E - Activation and orders resolution for side B. A can do opportunity reactions. repeat sequence D.I to D.V
2.F - End of turn - all orders completed
2.G - End of turn and start of next turn (2.A)

It is designed for the seasoned game-lover, continuously learning leader, and business student alike. There is more than one way to learn something by playing this game. All of the strategies in this game are real and promote best practices in Organization Development. Read each strategy description as you introduce new strategies to the game.

Imagine you are the CEO of your new start-up, a strategy guru, ready create the direction for your organization. Your industry is complex and changing. Just last year, the biggest player in the industry unexpectedly collapsed. The first order of business is to gain awareness about your Management, Environment, Communications, Innovation, and Culture. These are five of the most important influences on strategy.

The game itself mirrors a common strategic planning exercise in the business world: the SWOT Analysis. SWOT stands for Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats and the exercise is designed to increase your awareness of your internal and external environment. A good SWOT Analysis then takes the following critical step. You must take that newfound awareness and implement useful strategies for your organization.

But beware, no competitive advantage lasts forever—Except the ability to adapt. Welcome to the world of business psychology and strategic planning.

May the best strategies win!

—description from the designer

The principle
The turning point

The epilogue

In Rebisworld the whole process takes about 60 minutes. For each of the 4 parts the estimated time is about 10 minutes for the realization of the three writings and 5 minutes for the vote.

At the end the winner is the one who gets the most number of elected parts of history.

Each player receives one of eight recipe card which details their personal goal for the game.  A recipe card has the name of the recipe (e.g. Oatmeal Raisin Cookies) and five ingredients the player will be searching for (e.g. sugar, flour, oatmeal & raisins, butter, and eggs).  The deck of 36 ingredient cards is shuffled and placed face-down in the middle of the play area, where it's scattered to make all cards accessible.

Play is simple and chaotic.  Players grab at cards, one at a time, from the center and look at them.  If they need the card, they keep it.  If they don't, they put it back.

Among the cards are four "rotten egg" cards.  If a player draws a rotten egg, play temporarily stops while the player who drew it mixes all their collected ingredients back into the center.  Once done, play resumes as normal.

This continues until one player has collected all five ingredients.

SQUILLO CITY (CALL GIRL CITY)
ITALY’S BEST SELLING SATYRICAL CARD GAME IS BACK WITH EVEN MORE CRUELTY AND EVEN LESS POLITICAL CORRECTNESS!

The game is set in the city of Milangeles where sex and cultural stereotypes serve as a backdrop for an all-out war amongst the city’s prostitute factions - Russians, Asians, Africans, Americans and Latinas. Pick your favorite and battle it out across town as you engage in shady business, sell yourself on the streets, or off your competitors.

How to play:
Each player’s turn is very quick and composed of two main phases: 
Movement (Mandatory) and Action (Optional)
Movement Phase: 
You must move your character to any new location along the line you’re currently on. The new location may not be occupied by another player.  
Action:
You may take a single action chosen from those on the new location you’re occupying. Alternatively, you may use any action ability present on one of your call girls’ cards as long as its symbol matches the one on the currently occupied location. 
The effects of each action are found on the game board or on the call girl cards. 
After you have taken your action, and if you do not wish to play cards from your hand, you may end your turn and the player on your left will be the next active player.

During your turn you may play as many cards as you like from your hand, as long as their symbols match those on the currently occupied location.

Winning the Game:
The game ends immediately when the last card from the Black Market is purchased or when the last Coin is drawn from the Coin Pool. The player with the most Coins is declared the winner. In case of a draw, the player with the most Call Girls wins - should this also result in a draw, the victory is shared amongst the winning players.

In Toy Story 4 you take turns picking cards from a common storage and immediately place them in front of you. The way the storage works is central to the game and where the real interaction takes place.

The cards show shelves with toys. The background behind the shelves is either green, yellow or blue. When you place the cards in front of you, i e build the shelves, you may have a shelf of each of the 3 colors.

Some toys get an extra bonus for being placed next to each other, other toys cannot be placed next to each other.
Usually you may only have one toy of each sort on a shelf so you will have to finish shelves during the game. When you finish a shelf you put it to your scoring pile and may start a new shelf of that color.

When the game ends you add up all points shown on the cards you in your scoring pile. Most Points win.

各プレイヤーは6つのアクションを駆使し、事業を進めていきます。ただし、このゲームでは全員が同じアクションを行います。すなわち、あなたが選んだアクションをほかの二人も行います。そして、他の二人が選んだアクションを…あなたも、行います。よって、強いアクションを先に行ったほうが良いとは限らないのです。

大切なのは、より自分にとって必要で、他の二人にとって無意味なアクションを選ぶことになります。また、アクションを選択したプレイヤーには特権というおまけがついてきますので、他人に選んでもらうより少しだけ有利になります。

アクションは以下の6つです。アクション同士の並び方やウラ、オモテは遊ぶたびに変わります。ウラとオモテで建物の効果が変わり、その並び方で建物のコストを構成する色や得点が変わる仕組みです。

・出荷：倉庫にある石油製品をお金と得点に換えます。もっとも効率の良い得点源です。あなただけが出荷したなら、ですが。

・建設：お金と労働者、倉庫にある石油製品を使って、アクションを強化し得点にもなる建物を建てます。同じ建物は、先に建てた人ほど高得点ですが、後から建てた人ほど安くなります。使い捨ての建物や、得点になるだけの建物もあります。

・倉庫：製油工場4箇所から1箇所を選び、そこにあるうち、最も数が少ない色のキューブが1色だったならば、その色のキューブすべてを倉庫へ運び込みます。

・製油：製油工場4箇所から1箇所を選び、そこにあるすべてのキューブを、4箇所に好きに分配します。

・採掘：公開されている4個１セットの原油のキューブ3セットから、望む1セットをあなたの製油工場1箇所に手に入れます。

・雇用：建設と採掘の際にいろんな仕事をしてくれる労働者(使い捨て)を手に入れます。

バッグから出てくるキューブの色こそランダムですが、ほかにはランダムな要素はありません。三人専用の、濃密なインタラクションをお楽しみください。

Override is an abstract game for two players. The goal is simple: get five in a row in your color. What makes it interesting is that you and your opponent each have the option of controlling each other's pieces rather than your own on any given turn.

All 30 pyramids begin off the 5×5 board. Each turn, a player may bring a piece onto an empty square on the board or move a piece that is already on the board one space orthogonally. Larger pieces can cover up smaller pieces of either color; same-size stacking and smaller-on-larger stacking is not permitted. Pieces covering up other pieces are fair game to move orthogonally just like solo pieces. Players may make these moves with either their own color or the opposing color. A group of two or three pieces on the same square count as the top color for purposes of the five-in-a-row goal. It is possible for a pyramid to be temporarily blocked in due to its being surrounded on four sides by larger pyramids. Players are not allowed to directly undo an opponent's last move, but they are allowed to create a very similar board-state. For instance, if there are four yellow pyramids in a row and the active player moves a medium yellow pyramid out of line, the next player is not allowed to move the medium yellow pyramid back into place, but is allowed to bring an identical medium yellow pyramid from off the board into that square.

The first player to create a line of five in a row, diagonally or orthogonally, wins the game. In the case of two lines of opposing colors simultaneously being created, the active player wins.

Requires a 5×5 grid and 30 pyramids: fifteen each in two colors.

—description from the designer

description:
"From the future me to the past me
Tomorrow, I am going on a journey.
It's a mysterious letter from the future."

The Journey of My Future Self, a.k.a., 明日、旅に出ます, is a solitaire game in which you are invited to go on a journey and follow the mysteries of a closed world. As you travel, you encounter incidents from the past, present, and future, all of which will be repeated over and over again. You have to know them all in order to solve them. Will you be able to solve the mysteries of this world?

In more detail, The Journey of My Future Self is a game of medium to short-term planning in which you have to constantly think about the best way to move your pawns, taking into account the numbers 1 to 6 that are randomly given and the numbers that are yet to be seen.

The small number of cards used in the game (30) makes it difficult to recover from mistakes, and a single mistake can make the game impossible to complete. For this reason, we encourage thought experiments (trial play). This is all part of the solitaire nature of the game, which is designed to be played slowly and deliberately, without disturbing other players, no matter how long you think. We want you to forget everything else and immerse yourself in the game!

You can manipulate the difficulty of the game to some extent by using any combination of "items" that change the basic rules. If you want to enjoy the atmosphere of the game, you can use more items to reduce the difficulty. If you are aiming for a solid high score, you can choose to barter the risks and benefits. This freedom of use makes the game replayable and flexible, allowing the player to adjust the difficulty according to their preferences.

—description from the publisher

description:
A card game inspired by a detective drama of the Showa era.

The player becomes one of the "five unique detectives" and investigates the case by allocating 24 hours each. Five cases are set at the same time, and a five-row, plot-type auction is held. Scores will be given to the 1st and 2nd place "in the time allotted" for each case. Do this for 3 hands.

Detectives work together to investigate the case, but often go to stand, play to seek credit, or settle with the culprit. As a result, you may get deeply involved in the incident and get seriously injured or you may be killed!　Moreover, even if you get a line of duty, the game is not over, and if you can get a line of duty at a good show, you can earn a high score. Produce your own line of duty scene well and decide "What's this !!!!"

The basis of the game is a "5-row, plot-type auction".　A total of 24 common cards will be given to the player as a hand, and one card will be placed in each of the five cases, for a total of five cards. After placing, reveal the cards, and after seeing the result, arrange the remaining 5 sheets in the same way and judge by the total value. Therefore, it is important to avoid competition as much as possible and get more cases efficiently, because it is possible to guess to some extent who wants which case with the first placement.

Then, shouldn't it be better to hide the information by arranging as few numbers as possible for the first placement? Of course, that's one strategy. However, if the numbers are the same, there is a rule that the one with the larger number placed on the first sheet wins, so it is not always advantageous to arrange the smaller numbers.　In addition, each person has a ticket called hero time, and you can win regardless of the number unless the heroes spend time fighting with each other. Where to use this hero time is also important.

The light of the beginning!
The gods who created the world next wanted faithful servants who worshiped themselves.
The gods breathed life into the soil that they created to resemble themselves, and five races were born in this world.

This is a war game depicting the rise and conflict of various races.
This is a strategic-level game that competes in how to invade territory, eliminate foreign enemies, and spread and maintain your own prints. Because of the narrowness of the map, pacifism does not work. Somewhere in the game, you will almost certainly get into war.

Shaun and Edo are childhood friends, and wish to become famous U-Tube stars! They love posting hot dog eating live feeds, video games, and boring videos. Today, they were having trouble shooting a video in the garage of Shaun's grandpa's house. (By the way, how long can you withstand a bunch of live crayfish in your body?) But that has nothing to do with this story. Instead, everyday life had collapsed due to a sudden zombie pandemic. The dead resurrected and ate the living. As the evacuation activities proceeded, the two thought, "Now is our chance to become top U-Tubers? Let's film a zombie and upload it!" A fool is a big deal in a small town. Thus, the two idiots headed to the city center full of zombies. Shaun packed Grandpa’s keepsake revolver and ammunition in his backpack, as well as a smart phone and a lot of donuts. In this way, two stupid people began to stream an endless battle with the zombie pandemic to the world. "Share zombie videos with the world and become famous! I'm getting out of this shabby town!"

A cooperative zombie defense game, balance the life of your four barriers against the need to shoot and film the attacking zombies. Internet fame awaits!

language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Find your connections to various characters with this family friendly strategy game! Whether you are Mennonite or not, you will find this game fun and entertaining!

" The Mennonites play a game predictably called "The Mennonite Game," in which people try and discover how they might be connected. For example, I might find out that you were my brother's roommate's sister's ex-boyfriend's high school teacher. The game that I have created is a card game inspired by the Mennonite game, thus the title "The Mennonite Game Card Game."" - Description by the designer

Each person begins with a Starter Card, which is you! The object of the game is to be the first to collect 10 Character Cards.

The Character Cards you acquire branch off of the Starter Card to form your character tree. The trick of the game is collect characters that benefit you, while also fitting into your character tree.

You gain characters by turning in Connection Cards. There are 4 different types, and a wild card that can be used for any one connection type.

The third type of card is the Event Card. These cards can be played at any time during your turn to draw cards, block someone's Character tree, remove effects, and much more!

Run your very own paper company branch, while navigating the shifting economy, hiring employees, and maintaining your branch's morale.

In Paper Company, the economy is determined for all players based on a single die roll each round. The higher the roll is, the better the economy and the stronger each players' actions will perform that round. Once the economy is determined, all players choose 1 of 5 actions based on the roll:

     Accounting: To gain money
     Purchasing: To buy paper
     Sales: To sell paper
     Human Resources: To increase morale
     Recruiting: To hire or fire employees


By acquiring employees you can increase the benefits for specific actions, gain benefits when other players perform matching actions, and obtain abilities unique to that employee. However, be cautious who you hire, as employees have minds of their own always triggering their abilities, whether you want them to or not.  You must also be wary of how you treat your employees, as each branch has unique benefits, or detriments, based on how your employees feel about your management style.

With 5 unique branches and 40 unique employees, every game plays out differently. How will you manage your unique branch? Will you focus on one action, allowing other players to take advantage of your repetition? Will you get and keep morale high to reap the benefits of happy, productive employees, or try to make money quickly and work around the consequences? Will you focus on recruiting and fill your office with as many employees as you can, or will you try to avoid employees altogether and run the office by yourself?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Battle Toons: The House Of Jive
description:
In the city of Kokomo, the ruffian know as “The Jazz Man” has opened up a shady casino called The House Of Jive. The residential toons of Kokomo aren’t too happy about this and try to get the casino shut down. They however do not succeed as The Jazz Man is protected with the magic of “The Great Kokkino” and the might and insanity of “Jack De Lune”. Our heroes “Vega O’ Dar”, “Darla Dwolf”, and “Coyote Pete” have been alerted by The Jazz Man’s actions and are teaming up with YOU to stop him and get his casino shut down! With the magic of The Great Kokkino, he is holding all of these other toon’s against their will and are being forced to fight and protect the casino. Will you defeat The Jazz Man? Or will you succumb to his game?

—description from the designer
Two storm metal coins with rules to use them as a small expansion bound to the territory connection and the vulnerability of climate change.

This is a 100 card expansion with 10 themes voted on by the Kickstarter backers. It contains 10 cards from the following categories:

     Sci-fi
     Post-Apocalyptic
     Majestic Creatures
     Gods
     Planet Earth
     The '90's
     Cryptids
     Tom
     Backer-Submitted Cards
     Trolley Tom Story Cards

boardgame: Trial by Trolley: Derailed Bonus Pack
description: A 25-card bonus back for Trial by Trolley

boardgame: Joking Hazard: Trial by Trolley Promo Pack
description: This pack of 20 cards was included as Kickstarter-exclusive promo content for the Trial by Trolley Kickstarter campaign.

Mum-Mum Margaret: Bronze Leaf is a two-player game that is semi-handmade by a team of artisans. Each player takes turns to draw colorful petals and place them onto a spot with the same symbol. If you follow a certain order to put petals, you can place multiple of them in one turn for more points. Billy the honey bee will fly around each turn from a flower to another. Placing a petal onto the spot where he is stopped will activate special effect(s) depending on the definition of the flower he is stopping. The player with the most points wins the game.

ZENタイル ソロ (ZEN Tiles Solo) is a solitaire board game that challenges you to look at yourself objectively while placing emotion tiles on a 24-hour timeline.

To win, you need to find a spot to place twenty different emotion tiles above these time boards, so think carefully about "your yesterday". You might have become happy about yourself — "I had a positive thoughts!" — or were perhaps surprised: "I didn't realize that I have negative feelings every time when I see this person."

"Knowing yourself" is one of the most important processes to find a way to make our life better, and it is ZEN. Giving five minutes each day to yourself to play this game alone in a quiet place and listen to your soul could be good meditation for the people who feel tired every day.

ZEN Tile Solo is a game through which you can learn your hidden emotions and organize them to make your life calmer and better.
publisher's summary

ZENタイル ソロ (Zen Tiles Solo) published in 2019, has wooden components in a small, silver foil stamped box. It is a one player game. There are 20 Emotion Tiles, a time track and 1 Elegant Stone. The rules are in English and Japanese, with both languages shown on the Emotion Tiles. ZenTiles are from Fukui Prefecture, where the Eiheiji Temple, also known as the birthplace of Zen, is located.
user summary

boardgame: Master of Wills: Edgehunter Expansion Faction Deck
description:
Edgehunter brings new elemental powers to the game. Cast out from society they are forced to wander the wastelands looking to survive. When all hope seems lost, they stumble upon a lost artifact that imbues the survivors with elemental powers long forgotten. These new magical abilities cause these outcasts to ban together forming the faction of Edgehunter. With these new powers they will fight for their rightful place.

The world of chaos has bled over into all streams of life. Influence continues to shift from Faction to Faction with no end in sight. New powers have emerged that are shared between all Factions but how they are used is yet to be determined. Combining these technologies with their elemental powers, Edgehunters cans trike and take back their place in the world.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Punctuation Pirates and Their Grammar Galleons
description:
Punctuation Pirates and Their Grammar Galleons is the first game created by Robert Zumbrun and James R. Orr, a games created by teachers for students, to use in the classroom. This game has been a longtime passion for both these teachers, first proto-typed in 2011 and used in the classroom since It has been extensively played by students and teachers alike, and so much fun and joy has come from gameplay. This print-and-play version is for used for all teachers, students, or any who enjoy a good game of piratey fun.

A game of piracy, adventure on the high seas, and historical facts and fun about pirates. Fun for all those who know everything is  better with pirates, even if pirates are grammatically incorrect.

—description from the publisher

Are you ready for high seas adventure?

Gather yer crew an’ stow yer gear, because pirates be comin’. It be time for an adventure! Pirates be ruthless. Pirates be blood-thirsty. An’ pirates also be grammatically incorrect!

In Punctuation Pirates you are a pirate sailing around the sea, gathering treasure, engaging in battle, and learning all sorts of piratical facts. But pirates have a problem and are seldom understood. It is your charge to help them along the way by correcting their grammar.

Punctuation Pirates is an educational game for 2-6 players (or groups). It is filled with many historical facts about pirates and sea-faring things. It incorporates English, history, math, geography and science with its numerous different cards. Fun for any family or any classroom.

Welcome to the world of Punctuation Pirates!

—description from the back of the box

boardgame: Barkham Horror: The Card Game – The Meddling of Meowlathotep: Scenario Pack
description:
Barkham Horror is an alternate universe in which the conflict between humanity and the eldritch forces of the Mythos takes a back seat, and the conflict between dogs and cats takes center stage. In Barkham Horror: The Card Game – The Meddling of Meowlathotep, a 78-card standalone scenario pack, the investigators must stop Meowlathotep, the Prowling Chaos, Meowsenger of the Outer Feline Gods, who is terrorizing the city of Barkham. Only a few precious pups can defeat the various Meowsks of Meowlathotep and prevent them from destroying Barkham and the world!

When this adventure kicks off, you are hot on the trail of a cat conspiracy. A catspiracy, if you will. Dogs all across town report seeing strange, unnatural cats prowling the streets of Barkham, and each day more and more pigeons are going missing. You've picked up the scent of something big, and once you sink your teeth into a story, you just can't let go. A little wet fur has never stopped you from finding the truth. Perhaps if you investigate the areas of Arkham most plagued by these sightings, you can root out the cat‐monsters that dwell within.

—description from the publisher

description:
In 1953 a group of teenagers in a small town in Kansas stopped an alien invasion from Pluto. Now the aliens want to try again, but this time they are plotting to abduct the teenagers so the next invasion can proceed unhindered by those pesky kids!

Players take the role of the aliens in this cooperative game, following in the events of the first game Pluto Attacks. This time the aliens need to resolve a number of town folk and locations to gain the skills they need to abduct the teenagers.

This is a stand alone game set in the same game world as Pluto Attacks.

Whoa Nellie College Football Game is a new opportunity to recreate a realistic college football game!  Based on real statistics, you can pit teams from the past 50 years against each other in both solo and 2-player game play.

Take control of a team and run the ball with the top 4 rushers or throw the ball to the top 6 pass-catchers.  Their yards per carry/reception are used, as are their contribution to the offense.  Call any of 7 different defenses to stop your opponent.  Interceptions, sacks, and strategic use of the "Whoa Nellie" big play card to come out on top!

Whoa Nellie College Football provides appealing team cards, dice, and the components needed to enjoy exploring a recent team vs one from yesteryear or even pit co-national champions against each other to reveal who was truly better!

—description from the designer
Features 300 brand new cards and a new scoresheet.

A 3-card set of bonus abilities for Pickpockets.

Description from the publisher:

The bonus cards are 3 special ability cards featuring business cards of The Cleaner, Kingpin and The Pigeon.


To play with these new bills you will swap out the bill behind the business card. The card count will be exactly as before, but with the alternate bill in place. We suggest that you shuffle the alternate bill and the one being swapped out so you don't 100% know if the alternate bills are in place.

The Cleaner counts as a regular $10 bill, but also has the bonus ability of canceling a marked bill.

The Kingpin stops a Snitch from saying they are a cop. You take an unmarked bill of your choice from their end THEN the game ends. The player with the most money wins.

The Pigeon is an ally to the Snitch / Cop in 4 player games. The player who holds the Pigeon card cannot snitch, but only wins if the cop wins. On the downside, it’s a little harder for the cop to win since the Pigeon’s marked bills don’t count.

boardgame: A Luminous Flame in The Darkness
description:
A Luminous Flame in The Darkness (LFID) is innovative Board Game for sharing and learning. The LFID is Homemade Integrated Design. It is also suitable for a solo game at home.  The player can share and learn about ethic, goodness, moral, and core cultures while they adventure and explore with many experiences on the map of a community. Some happenings tend to be worse. How do they live? How do they make a decision? or How do they take an action?

Each Player can create Core Culture and a story together, including modify rules. In the game, the player may see a picture emerge of inter-relationships with multiple factors that are connected, such as causes that lead to multiple degenerations or the existence of individual forces against outer contexts. Preservation and recovery of life is more difficult than destruction. Creativity, life, people and the maintenance are essential for the existence of a core culture. The environment is not for the individual to exploit, but for all lives to enjoy.  This game simulates incidents happening in a world of fantasy in which virtue and ethics are crucial to the survival of humanity.  Each player will be able to perceive an overall concept of the core culture which they created together in the game.

    Note : In case of using for eduacional game, an experienced adult or parent is required to teach and guide.


BACKGROUND :
I graduated in Ph.D. of Arts and Culture Research from the Faculty of Fine Arts of Srinakarinwirot University in Bangkok, Thailand. My research of Green Culture for Ethics Through Documentary Films, found out the issues of many problems and wide ranging effects, including the realisation that the relationship between natures and ethics are complementary in both the individual and public contexts. The complimentary nature of living in accordance with an ecological system leads to a balance between the physical and subjective, the concrete and the abstract, human behaviour and the mind which can be seen as the foundation of ethics for peaceful and sustainable mutual living.

A Game Story :
After humanity has destroyed nature and the environment in many areas, some of the areas have  been restored and rejuvenated. Nevertheless, many people turn to violence to hurt and destroy each other for fame and wealth, focusing on dominating the land which causes many problems and adverse living conditions.  People have lost trust for each other : no faithfulness exists. They have lived in unsafe place and lead insecure lives. At the same time that pollution has dominated and deterioration also spreads widely into several areas.  In one remote district, a city that was once full of happiness, many things have began to change. However, the last thing that still keeps faith and hope and that will not be destroyed so easily, the GOODNESS OF PEOPLE

OBJECTIVE
All players help most members in their community to stay away from evil power before most terrible things appear. They try to cultivate goodness and ethics in the land of loss before the darkness comes.  At the end of game, all players consider successful future and present outcomes together.

GENRE
Fantasy game, 
Solo Game,
Co-operative game, 
Strategic game, 
and Educational game

—description from the designer

Towers & War Machines
Employ crafty engineers to build ingenious, death-bringing machines, and to raise towers with slots for them!

Each player will be able to build towers (they are located between Wall Sections) and place chosen war machines onto them. There are three variants of War Machines: cannons, rocket batteries, and powerful siege crossbows.

Not only you will be able to use them in the game, greatly enhancing your defense potential, but also, each player will get a few upgrade cards to choose from, giving further variation and replay possibilities to the War Machines. For example, one player might favor building cannons, but they want them to be as cheap as possible, while another might concentrate on a single but very powerful one.

Additional Horde types & Siege Engines
Mongols have brought some siege engines with them! Some of the Hordes cards will come with the special engines to try and break your walls. Defend yourself against battering rams, which will break your walls, catapults, which will strike your archers, and Hordes with siege towers, which will change the way you plan your defense. They will try and counteract your towers, employing miners to tear them down.

New Hordes will add to the replayability while being able to be used in basic games as well.

New Troops!
Each Clan sends out their elite warriors to join the fray - all of the Clans will have their own unique and extremely powerful Soldier! You can upgrade one of your basic Soldier types into an elite. For example, Snake Clan will be upgrading their Archers into Chu-Ko-Nu Crossbowmen, being able to outshoot any of the opponents quickly. On the other hand, Dragon Clan, as befits their name, will equip some of their Spearmen with fire-lances. You can be sure they won’t be cheap, but if you can get them recruited early in the game, you will gain a huge advantage over the Hordes (and your opponents).

New Generals, Advisor, Artefacts and Tech-Trees!
This expansion brings also new Generals, Advisors and Artifacts to further enhance replayability and allow for bigger customization of your desired tactics.

Additionally, this expansion will introduce Tech-Trees creating new exciting possibilities of development for each clan. Whit those cards you will be able to adjust your strategy and invest into Towers, War Machines or enhanced Army units!

—description from the publisher

This special addon consists of 1 big miniature, 1 mini board and deck of 5 cards.

The Dragon is the weapon of last resort, to use if you see that you won’t be able to hold a Wall. Using it carries great danger, but usually positive effects will overshadow the negatives.

If you want to use Iron Dragon you will have to assign Clerk to it. First player will have to spend resources to cast the cannon, but will get to choose on which Wall Section it will be deployed. It is obvious, that Emperor will bestow great Honor on the one, who created such a great weapon. Then, all players can send their Clerk to shoot from the Iron Dragon. When player shoots, they draw 1 card from mini-deck, showing what effect the shot had. Effects vary from blowing huge hole in the Horde card, through just scaring them off for a turn, to cannon exploding and destroying current level of the wall.

If you do not like the random effect you can play that you can choose the effect - even exploding it sometimes, so other player cannot use it or you can build it (and earn Honor for doing so) yourself.

—description from the publisher

Included in the book EBA 5.0, which commemorates the 50th anniversary of the publication of A Esmorga, by Eduardo Blanco Amor. Tres de troula is a board game that takes you through the world of this story.

Each player will become a character from A Esmorga, with its different characteristics, which will give them bonus on dice rolls. They will have to find a balance between drunkenness and thought.

The board is discovered as you move through it, turning around the square you fall into. The condition of victory is to get home first, after a night of partying.

—user summary
